Standards of Care (SoC) — Miro gap analysis

81 UAT test cases checked against AC Flows — Standards of Care (Miro). 11 candidate gaps · 0 high. Intent, full chat & expected results taken from each test case; alignment is heuristic — confirm against the canvas.

—Intent: expenses after a disruption → reimbursementcore81

SOC-01"I can help you create a claim for your expenses … see if you are eligible for reimbursement"core25

GenUC-05Authenticationcore81
ADD-99Is PNR a group booking? → Yes: skip passenger selection (self only)branch79
GenUC-06Passenger Selection (multi-pax)branch3
GenUC-07Multi-Pax Permissionbranch0
GenUC-08Journey Selectioncore81
GenUC-18Segment Selection (>1 segment)branch81
GenUC-10Duplicate Claim Checkbranch81
SoC-02a · ADD-98Passed expected arrival of last itinerary? → No: come back after arrival → Endbranch79
SoC-02a · GenUC-25 · ADD-83>72h for all segments? → No: 72-hour queue + email confirm → End (PENDING)branch81
ADD-105Triage data available? → No: missing info → GenUC-27 MVP Manual Handoff (do NOT upload receipts)branch74
SoC-02bTriage — Eligibility checkcore81
SOC-03cAC & STAR/Partner → process AC + forward Star to agentbranch3
SOC-03dAC & OAL → AC portion only; contact the other airlinebranch1
SOC-03eSTAR/Partner only → forward to agent for reviewbranch3
SOC-03fSTAR/Partner & OAL → forward Star; contact OAL for the restbranch8
SOC-03gOAL only → "reach out to [airline] directly" → End (NO DETERMINATION)branch12
SoC-03b · ADD-23 · GLOB-05ELIGIBLE → Fraud Screening → "you may be eligible for reimbursement"branch81
RR-01Receipt Upload Flow (one at a time; non-itemized/CC statements rejected) → OCRbranch81
SoC-03bNOT ELIGIBLE — delay < 2 hours → not entitledbranch59
SoC-03b · ADD-27/106NOT ELIGIBLE — ineligible delay (weather / outside airline control)branch59
GenUC-15Dispute Flow ("I think this is a mistake!")branch48
GenUC-16/19Claim Paymentbranch81
GenUC-27Manual Handling (collect receipts)branch27
GLOB-11End Flowcore80

SoC-APPR-NE-01 · Not Eligible — APPR – Not Eligible – Employee Booking AC
APPR NE dispute employee h72
💬 Customer intent from test case
👤 My flight was delayed for several hours and I had to buy food at the airport and book a hotel for the night. Can I get reimbursed for what I spent?
🗺️ Canonical Miro path — coverage (Standards of Care / SoC)
✓ asserted · ✕ required-by-Miro but missing · · not applicable
✓ GLOB-01✓ —✓ GenUC-23· GLOB-19 · ADD-112✓ GenUC-24✕ SOC-01✓ GenUC-01✓ GenUC-05· ADD-99· GenUC-06· GenUC-07✓ GenUC-08✓ GenUC-18✓ GenUC-10· SoC-02a · ADD-98✓ SoC-02a · GenUC-25 · ADD-83· ADD-105✓ SoC-02b· SOC-03c· SOC-03d· SOC-03e· SOC-03f· SOC-03g✓ SoC-03b · ADD-23 · GLOB-05✓ RR-01✓ SoC-03b✓ SoC-03b · ADD-27/106✓ GenUC-15✓ GenUC-16/19✓ GenUC-27✓ GLOB-11

💬 Test-case conversation from test case (Detailed Steps)
🤖
Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?
English
👤
🤖
Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice.
🤖
How can I help you today?
My flight was delayed for several hours and I had to buy food at the airport and book a hotel for the night. Can I get reimbursed for what I spent?
👤
⚙️ the chatbot identifies Standards of Care (SoC) intent
⚙️ the chatbot checks: Is this a third-party submission? → NO
🤖
Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd liketo be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited. Would you like to get started?
🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.
🤖
Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable.
[First Name] [Last Name]
👤
🤖
Thanks, [First Name]! And, what is your booking reference number?
PNR: [PNR Code]

• Email: t***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***789

EXPECTED RESULTS
⚙️ System correctly identifies employee booking AFTER OTP authentication (not blocked at entry). Returns SoC-APPR-NE-01. Displays NOT ELIGIBLE. Offers dispute option — if disputed, case created and sent to manual handling. No receipt upload triggered.
✅ Expected results from test case (Expected + CDV + Payment)
PAYMENT VALIDATION:
📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify that when a customer has an AC employee standby/confirmed booking, the system returns NOT ELIGIBLE under APPR for Standards of Care and displays the appropriate ineligibility messaging.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: APPR
• Itinerary: Single segment — AC 101 YYZ→YUL
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 101 YYZ→YUL
• Promised Itinerary: AC 101 YYZ→YUL, Dep: 01-Jun 08:00
• Actual Itinerary: AC 101 YYZ→YUL, Dep: 01-Jun 14:00
• Departure Delay: 360 min (6 hours)
• Departure delay code: 64 (Flight Crew Shortage) — Controllable
• Controllability: Controllable (Code 64)
• Passenger Type: Employee (AC) – office suffix ES/OT/EP/EE/EC
• Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Employee → NOT ELIGIBLE (no further checks)
• PAX Count: 1 adult
• Class of Booking: PY
• Revenue Booking: NO – Employee booking
• ACR Issued: N/A
• Journey Completed: > 72h
• Employee Flow: Employee is NOT blocked upfront. Goes through OTP authentication first, then receives "Not Eligible" response.
• Dispute Path: If disputed → case created → sent to manual handling.
• Policy: Not eligible under CCT (decided by Mohammed). Eligible for baggage claims only.
• Note on Exception Pax: If this were a J cabin exception/priority customer, a meal entitlement at J cabin rate would apply — but employee check overrides all exception pax logic first.

• Regime: APPR
• Itinerary: Single segment — AC 101 YYZ→YUL
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 101 YYZ→YUL
• Promised Itinerary: AC 101 YYZ→YUL, Dep: 01-Jun 08:00
• Actual Itinerary: AC 101 YYZ→YUL, Dep: 01-Jun 14:00
• Departure Delay: 360 min (6 hours)
• Departure delay code: 64 (Flight Crew Shortage) — Controllable
• Controllability: Controllable (Code 64)
• Passenger Type: Employee (AC) – office suffix ES/OT/EP/EE/EC
• Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Employee → NOT ELIGIBLE (no further checks)
• PAX Count: 1 adult
• Class of Booking: PY
• Revenue Booking: NO – Employee booking
• ACR Issued: N/A
• Journey Completed: > 72h
• Employee Flow: Employee is NOT blocked upfront. Goes through OTP authentication first, then receives "Not Eligible" response.
• Dispute Path: If disputed → case created → sent to manual handling.
• Policy: Not eligible under CCT (decided by Mohammed). Eligible for baggage claims only.
• Note on Exception Pax: If this were a J cabin exception/priority customer, a meal entitlement at J cabin rate would apply — but employee check overrides all exception pax logic first.

I've reviewed the details of your flight. I understand your departure from Toronto was delayed by 18 hours. However, you are not eligible for expense reimbursement for this delay. After a full review, we can confirm that you were informed of the transit delay prior, 15 hours of the scheduled departure.

PAYMENT VALIDATION:
📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify that when the SOC segment is operated by an OAL carrier (no AC involvement on the disrupted segment), the system returns NOT ELIGIBLE and directs the customer to the operating airline.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: APPR
• Itinerary: Single segment — UA 456 ORD→LAX 
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): UA 456 ORD→LAX (OAL only — AC has no data)
• Promised Itinerary: UA 456 ORD→LAX, Dep: 02-Jun 10:00
• Actual Itinerary: UA 456 ORD→LAX, Dep: 02-Jun 15:00
• Departure Delay: 300 min (5 hours)
• Departure delay code: N/A — OAL only, AC has no departure delay code data
• Controllability: N/A – OAL only
• Passenger Type: Regular
• Check Order: 1. Carrier check → OAL only → NOT ELIGIBLE (no AC involvement)
• PAX Count: 1 adult
• Class of Booking: PY
• Revenue Booking: YES
• ACR Issued: N/A
• Journey Completed: > 72h

TRIP TRACER UI VALIDATIONS
⚙️ the tester opens Trip Tracer UI for PNR
⚙️ ☐ Multi-segment domestic trip YYZ→YYC→YEG — Segment 2 AC602 YYC→YEG was delayed +5H 00M due to weather (uncontrollable)
⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Seg 1: AC 601, YYZ → YYC, Dep: 04-Jun 07:00 | Seg 2: AC 602, YYC → YEG, Dep: 04-Jun 12:00
⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Seg 1: AC 601, YYZ → YYC, Status: ON TIME | Seg 2: AC 602, YYC → YEG, Status: ARRIVED LATE, Scheduled Dep: 12:00 → Actual: 17:00, Delay: +5H 00M
⚙️ ☐ SOC section present — SOC segment: AC 602 YYC→YEG
⚙️ ☐ Intent: TRAVEL DISRUPTIONS | Sub-Intent: EXPENSES | Status: NOT ELIGIBLE (or PENDING if disputed → manual handling)
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-APPR-NE-07
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
🤖
I've reviewed the details of your flight. I understand your departure from Toronto was delayed by 5 hours. However, you are not eligible for expense reimbusement for this delay. After a full review, we can confirm that weather was the primary reason. Since the delay was caused by severe weather conditions. Weather and other factors are outside of the airline's control aren't covered under the compensation regulations APPR. If you have travel insurance, you may want to contact your insurance provider to see if your policy offers any coverage for this situation.
🤖
I think this is a mistake! I should be entitled to something!
DISPUTE FLOW
🤖
I understand your frustation [Name], we can confirm that weather was the primary reason. Since the delay was caused by severe weather conditions. Weather and other factors are outside of the airline's control aren't covered under the compensation regulations APPR. If yo have travel insurance, you may want to contact your insurance provider to see if your policy offers any coverage for this situation. You can learn more about our reimbursement policy for Weather disruption here : link
🤖
Is there anything else I can help you with today?
I want to speak with a person about this decision!
👤

PAYMENT VALIDATION:
📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify that when a regular (non-Priority) customer has a transit delay ≥2h deemed outside carrier control, the system returns NOT ELIGIBLE under APPR.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: APPR
• Itinerary: Multi-segment
- Segment 1: AC 601 YYZ→YYC, Dep: 04-Jun 07:00 (on time, no departure delay)
- Segment 2: AC 602 YYC→YEG, Dep: 04-Jun 12:00 (delayed)
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 602 YYC→YEG (Segment 2 — the segment with the qualifying departure delay)
• Promised Itinerary:
- Seg 1: AC 601 YYZ→YYC, Dep: 04-Jun 07:00
- Seg 2: AC 602 YYC→YEG, Dep: 04-Jun 12:00
• Actual Itinerary:
- Seg 1: AC 601 YYZ→YYC, Dep: 04-Jun 07:00 (on time)
- Seg 2: AC 602 YYC→YEG, Dep: 04-Jun 17:00 (5h departure delay)
• Departure Delay on SOC Segment (Seg 2): 300 min (5 hours)
• Departure delay code on SOC Segment: 77 (Weather) — Uncontrollable
• Controllability: Uncontrollable (Code 77)
• Passenger Type: Regular (not exception/priority)
• Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Regular (not exception) ✓ → 2. Controllability → Outside carrier control → NOT ELIGIBLE
• PAX Count: 1 adult
• Class of Booking: PY
• Revenue Booking: YES
• ACR Issued: N/A
• Journey Completed: > 72h

SOC_UAT-007SoC-APPR-NE-08Not EligibleAPPR – Not Eligible – No Travel Origin – Outside Carrier Control1 gap▾
SoC-APPR-NE-08 · Not Eligible — APPR – Not Eligible – No Travel Origin – Outside Carrier Control
APPR NE h72 notravel weather
💬 Customer intent from test case
👤 My flight was delayed for several hours and I had to buy food at the airport and book a hotel for the night. Can I get reimbursed for what I spent?
🗺️ Canonical Miro path — coverage (Standards of Care / SoC)
✓ asserted · ✕ required-by-Miro but missing · · not applicable
✓ GLOB-01✓ —✓ GenUC-23· GLOB-19 · ADD-112✓ GenUC-24✕ SOC-01✓ GenUC-01✓ GenUC-05✓ ADD-99· GenUC-06· GenUC-07✓ GenUC-08✓ GenUC-18✓ GenUC-10✓ SoC-02a · ADD-98✓ SoC-02a · GenUC-25 · ADD-83✓ ADD-105✓ SoC-02b· SOC-03c· SOC-03d· SOC-03e✓ SOC-03f· SOC-03g✓ SoC-03b · ADD-23 · GLOB-05✓ RR-01✓ SoC-03b✓ SoC-03b · ADD-27/106✓ GenUC-15✓ GenUC-16/19· GenUC-27✓ GLOB-11
⚠️ Gap analysis vs Miro · 1 low
LOWWeather / uncontrollable NE: Miro wording states the delay was outside the airline's control and is not covered under the regulation (ADD-27/ADD-106). Not asserted.

My flight was delayed for several hours and I had to buy food at the airport and book a hotel for the night. Can I get reimbursed for what I spent?

PAYMENT VALIDATION:
📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify not eligible when customer did not travel (remained at origin) and disruption was outside carrier control.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: APPR
• Itinerary: Single segment — AC 701 YYZ→YWG
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 701 YYZ→YWG
• Promised Itinerary: AC 701 YYZ→YWG, Dep: 05-Jun 06:00
• Actual Itinerary: Flight cancelled. Passenger stayed at origin (YYZ). No rebooking.
• Departure Delay: N/A – no travel (cancelled)
• Departure delay code: 72 (ATC restriction) — Uncontrollable
• Controllability: Uncontrollable (Code 72)
• Passenger Type: Regular
• Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Regular ✓ → 2. Controllability → Uncontrollable → NOT ELIGIBLE
• PAX Count: 1 adult
• Class of Booking: PY
• Revenue Booking: YES
• ACR Issued: N/A
• Journey Completed: > 72h

⚙️ the chatbot checks: Filing deadline for APPR SOC per province
⚙️ the system calculates:
• Origin: YYZ (Ontario) → Limitation = 730 days
• Destination: YVR (British Columbia) → Limitation = 1,095 days
• Days elapsed: Within limitation period → PROCEED

⚙️ ☐ Single-segment domestic trip YYZ→YVR on AC801 was diverted — returned to origin due to weather (uncontrollable)
⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Flight: AC 801, Route: YYZ → YVR, Scheduled Dep: 06-Jun 08:00
⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Flight: AC 801, Route: YYZ → YVR, Status: DIVERTED — Returned to origin (YYZ)
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-APPR-NE-10
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
🤖
I've reviewed the details of your flight. Unfortunately, your flight AC 801 from Toronto to Vancouver was diverted back to Toronto due to severe weather conditions. Under the applicable regulations (APPR), expense reimbursement is not available for disruptions caused by weather and other factors outside the airline's control. If you have travel insurance, you may want to contact your insurance provider to see if your policy offers any coverage for this situation.
🤖
Do you agree with this assessment, or would you like to dispute it?
• Agree
• Dispute
Agree
👤

🤖

• I would like you to – new flow
• No
No
👤
🤖
Thank you for choosing Air Canada.

PAYMENT VALIDATION:
📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify not eligible when customer was at mid-point, returned to origin, disruption outside carrier control.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: APPR
• Itinerary: Single segment — AC 801 YYZ→YVR
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 801 YYZ→YVR
• Promised Itinerary: AC 801 YYZ→YVR, Dep: 06-Jun 08:00
• Actual Itinerary: AC 801 departed YYZ, diverted back to YYZ. Passenger returned to origin.
• Departure Delay: N/A – no travel (returned to origin)
• Departure delay code: 77 (Weather) — Uncontrollable
• Controllability: Uncontrollable (Code 77)
• Passenger Type: Regular
• Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Regular ✓ → 2. Controllability → Uncontrollable → NOT ELIGIBLE
• PAX Count: 1 adult
• Class of Booking: PY
• Revenue Booking: YES
• ACR Issued: N/A
• Journey Completed: > 72h

• Regime: APPR
• Itinerary: Single segment — AC 801 YYZ→YVR
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 801 YYZ→YVR
• Promised Itinerary: AC 801 YYZ→YVR, Dep: 06-Jun 08:00
• Actual Itinerary: AC 801 departed YYZ, diverted back to YYZ. Passenger returned to origin.
• Departure Delay: N/A – no travel (returned to origin)

Under the applicable regulations (APPR), expense reimbursement is not available for disruptions caused by factors outside the airline's control. 📋 Prerequisites / Description
Same as NE-12 but RE used cancellation code.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: APPR
• Itinerary: Multi-segment
• Segment 1: AC 901 YYZ→YYC, Dep: 06-Jun 07:00 (operated normally)
• Segment 2: AC 902 YYC→YVR, Dep: 06-Jun 12:00 (cancelled)
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 902 YYC→YVR (Segment 2 — the cancelled segment)
• Promised Itinerary:
Seg 1: AC 901 YYZ→YYC, Dep: 06-Jun 07:00
Seg 2: AC 902 YYC→YVR, Dep: 06-Jun 12:00
• Actual Itinerary: Incomplete journey. Stranded at mid-point (YYC). Seg 2 cancelled.
• Departure Delay: N/A – no travel on SOC segment
• Departure delay code: not available
• Cancellation code: ARPT (Airport closure/restrictions) — Uncontrollable

• Class of Booking: PY
• Revenue Booking: YES

My flight was cancelled and I was rebooked on a later flight, but the rebooking was only about 2 and a half hours later. I still had to buy food while waiting. Can I get reimbursed for what I spent?

Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited. Would you like to get started?
🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.

• Origin: YYZ (Ontario) → Limitation = 730 days
• Destination: YUL (Quebec) → Limitation = 1,095 days

⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Flight: AC 111, Route: YYZ → YUL, Status: CANCELLED — Rebooked, SOC Segment Departure Delay: +1H 30M
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-APPR-NE-14
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
🤖
I've reviewed the details of your flight. Your flight AC 111 from Toronto to Montreal was cancelled and you were rebooked on a later flight. However, the total delay to your journey was 1 hour and 30 minutes, which is below the minimum 2-hour threshold required for expense reimbursement under the applicable regulations (APPR).

• Agree
• Dispute
Agree
👤

I've reviewed the details of your flight. Our records show that flight AC 141 from Toronto to Halifax operated as scheduled and was not cancelled. Since the flight was not disrupted, expense reimbursement is not available under the applicable regulations (APPR). If you chose not to travel for personal reasons, you may want to review your ticket's fare rules for refund or rebooking options.

💬 Customer intent from test case

🗺️ Canonical Miro path — coverage (Standards of Care / SoC)

🤖
How can I help you today?
I had a connecting flight booked through Air Canada. The first leg on Air Canada was fine, but the second leg operated by United Airlines was delayed by 4 hours. I had to pay for food and a hotel. Can I get reimbursed?
👤
⚙️ the chatbot identifies Standards of Care (SoC) intent

Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited. Would you like to get started?
🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.

Yes

⚙️ ☐ Multi-segment trip YYZ→ORD→SFO — Segment 1 AC789 on time, Segment 2 UA8765 (STAR) delayed +4H 00M
⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Seg 1: AC 789, YYZ → ORD, Dep: 02-Jun 08:00 | Seg 2: UA 8765, ORD → SFO, Dep: 02-Jun 14:00
⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Seg 1: AC 789, YYZ → ORD, Status: ON TIME | Seg 2: UA 8765, ORD → SFO, Status: ARRIVED LATE, Scheduled Dep: 14:00 → Actual: 18:00, Delay: +4H 00M
⚙️ ☐ SOC section present — SOC Segment: UA 8765 ORD→SFO (STAR)
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-APPR-ND-02
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC & STAR
NO DETERMINATION RESULT
🤖
I've reviewed the details of your journey. The disruption on your trip was caused by a delay on your United Airlines flight UA 8765 from Chicago to San Francisco, which was operated by a Star Alliance partner. Since this segment was not operated by Air Canada, we are unable to determine your eligibility for expense reimbursement automatically at this time. We have created a case for our team to review. In the meantime, please have any receipts for expenses you incurred during the disruption ready, as our team may request them during the review.
RECEIPT COLLECTION INITIATED

• Departure Delay: 360 min (6 hours)
• Departure delay code: NONE (not populated after 14-day retrigger)
• Controllability: Unknown – no departure delay code available
• Passenger Type: Regular
• Check Order: 1. Departure delay code check → NONE → No Determination
• 14-Day RE Retrigger: Triggered and completed, still no code
• PAX Count: 1 adult
• Class of Booking: PY
• Revenue Booking: YES
• ACR Issued: N/A
• Journey Completed: > 72h (well beyond 14 days)

⚙️ the tester opens Trip Tracer UI for PNR
⚙️ ☐ Single-segment domestic trip YYZ→YVR on AC501 was cancelled due to aircraft defect (safety)

🤖
Great news! Based on the details of your flight, you are eligible for expense reimbursement. Your flight AC 501 from Toronto to Vancouver was cancelled due to an aircraft safety issue, and you were unable to travel for approximately 24 hours. Under the applicable regulations (APPR), you may be reimbursed for reasonable expenses incurred during this disruption.
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY
🤖

PAYMENT VALIDATION:
📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify that when customer did not travel (remained at origin) following a safety disruption, system returns ELIGIBLE under APPR and proceeds to receipt upload and case creation.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: APPR
• Itinerary: Single segment — AC 501 YYZ→YVR
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 501 YYZ→YVR
• Promised Itinerary: AC 501 YYZ→YVR, Dep: 10-Jun 07:00
• Actual Itinerary: Flight cancelled. Passenger stayed at origin (YYZ). No rebooking within 24h.
• Departure Delay: 1440 min (24 hours)
• Departure delay code: 41(TD — Aircraft Defects or Maintenance Finding) — Safety
• Controllability: Safety (Code 41)
• Passenger Type: Regular
• Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Regular ✓ → 2. Controllability → Safety → ELIGIBLE
• PAX Count: 1 adult
• Class of Booking: PY
• Revenue Booking: YES
• ACR Issued: Hotel: No, Transport: No, Meal: No
• Journey Completed: > 72h
• ENTITLEMENTS (, 1 adult, PY, no ACR):
- Meals: $60/pax
- Hotel: Rate of day, max 3 nights, $500 cap (overnight eligible — delay >360 min)
- Transport: $200 (1–5 pax)
- Communication: $30/pax
- Parking: $50, 1 day max
- Essentials: $30/pax

• Regime: APPR

SoC-APPR-EL-02 · Eligible — APPR – Eligible – No Travel Origin – Safety (Fallback Code)
APPR EL h72 notravel safety
💬 Customer intent from test case
👤 My flight was cancelled due to a mechanical issue and I couldn't travel. I was rebooked the next day. I had to pay for food, a hotel, and a taxi. Can I get reimbursed?
🗺️ Canonical Miro path — coverage (Standards of Care / SoC)
✓ asserted · ✕ required-by-Miro but missing · · not applicable
✓ GLOB-01✓ —✓ GenUC-23· GLOB-19 · ADD-112✓ GenUC-24✓ SOC-01✓ GenUC-01✓ GenUC-05✓ ADD-99· GenUC-06· GenUC-07✓ GenUC-08✓ GenUC-18✓ GenUC-10✓ SoC-02a · ADD-98✓ SoC-02a · GenUC-25 · ADD-83✓ ADD-105✓ SoC-02b· SOC-03c· SOC-03d· SOC-03e· SOC-03f· SOC-03g✓ SoC-03b · ADD-23 · GLOB-05✓ RR-01· SoC-03b· SoC-03b · ADD-27/106· GenUC-15✓ GenUC-16/19· GenUC-27✓ GLOB-11

✅ No divergences detected against the Miro flow (heuristic — confirm against the canvas).

🤖
Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?
English
👤
🤖
Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice.
🤖
How can I help you today?
My flight was cancelled due to a mechanical issue and I couldn't travel. I was rebooked the next day. I had to pay for food, a hotel, and a taxi. Can I get reimbursed?
👤
⚙️ the chatbot identifies Standards of Care (SoC) intent
⚙️ the chatbot checks: Is this a third-party submission? → NO
🤖
Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited. Would you like to get started?
🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.
🤖
Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable.
[First Name] [Last Name]
👤

Thanks, [First Name]! And, what is your booking reference number?
PNR: [PNR Code]

• Email: t***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***789
Email: t***@gmail.com
👤

I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it.

🤖
Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step.

⚙️ the chatbot checks if there are multiple PAX on PNR? → No
🤖
I found the following journey: • Flight: AC 501 • Route: Toronto (YYZ) → Vancouver (YVR) • Date: June 10, 2026 • Status: CANCELLED – Aircraft defect Is this the journey you'd like to check?
Yes
👤
⚙️ the chatbot checks if there are more than 1 segment → No
⚙️ the chatbot sends request to Case Services for duplicate check
⚙️ Case Services returns: NO existing case found for this passenger + journey combination
⚙️ the chatbot checks if it passed the expected Departure time of the last itinerary → Yes
⚙️ the chatbot checks has it been more than 72 hours for all segments in the selected journey → Yes
CLAIM TIME LIMIT CHECK
⚙️ the chatbot checks: Filing deadline for APPR SOC per province
⚙️ the system calculates:
• Origin: YYZ (Ontario) → Limitation = 730 days
• Destination: YVR (British Columbia) → Limitation = 1,095 days
• Days elapsed: Within limitation period → PROCEED
⚙️ the chatbot checks if there is triage data available? → Yes
TRIAGE & DDS EVALUATION
⚙️ the DDS evaluation is done in backend
⚙️ DDS Evaluates and sends to Trip Tracer UI:
TRIP TRACER UI VALIDATIONS
⚙️ the tester opens Trip Tracer UI for PNR
⚙️ ☐ Single-segment domestic trip YYZ→YVR on AC501 was cancelled — departure delay code empty, cancellation code PERF (safety fallback)
⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Flight: AC 501, Route: YYZ → YVR, Scheduled Dep: 10-Jun 07:00
⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Flight: AC 501, Route: YYZ → YVR, Status: CANCELLED
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-APPR-EL-02
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
⚙️ the system sends case data to CyberSource for fraud screening (behind the scenes — not visible to passenger)
⚙️ CyberSource returns: GREEN (no fraud indicators detected)
⚙️ → PROCEED to eligible flow
🤖
Great news! Based on the details of your flight, you are eligible for expense reimbursement. Your flight AC 501 from Toronto to Vancouver was cancelled due to an aircraft safety issue, and you were unable to travel for approximately 24 hours. Under the applicable regulations (APPR), you may be reimbursed for reasonable expenses incurred during this disruption.
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY
🤖
Based on the length of your disruption (24 hours), you may be eligible for reimbursement in the following categories: • Meals: up to $60.00 • Hotel accommodation: Rate of day (up to 3 nights, $500 maximum) • Ground transportation: up to $200.00 • Communication: up to $30.00 • Parking: up to $50.00 (1 day maximum) • Essential items: up to $30.00 Please note that all expenses must have been incurred during the disruption period and supported by valid receipts.

• Departure Delay: 1440 min (24 hours)
• SOC Code Priority:
- 1st (Primary):  (departure delay codes) → EMPTY (flight cancelled, never departed — no departure delay codes exist)
- 2nd (Fallback): PERF (Performance/Safety Standards)
• Cancellation Code: PERF (Performance/Safety Standards)
• Controllability: Safety (via cancellation code PERF)
• Passenger Type: Regular
• Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Regular ✓ → 2. Delay Code→ NOT PRESENT → 3. Cancellation Code check → PERF → Safety → ELIGIBLE
• PAX Count: 1 adult
• Class of Booking: PY
• Revenue Booking: YES
• ACR Issued: Hotel: No, Transport: No, Meal: No
• Journey Completed: > 72h
• ENTITLEMENTS (1 adult, PY, no ACR):
- Meals: $60 / Hotel: Rate of day, 3 nights max, $500 cap / Transport: $200 / Communication: $30 / Parking: $50 / Essentials: $30

🤖
Great news! Based on the details of your flight, all 3 passengers on your booking are eligible for expense reimbursement. Your flight AC 601 from Toronto to Edmonton was diverted back to Toronto due to a mechanical safety issue, and your party was unable to travel for approximately 16 hours. Under the applicable regulations (APPR), you may be reimbursed for reasonable expenses incurred during this disruption.
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY
🤖
Based on the length of your disruption (16 hours) and your party size (3 passengers), you may be eligible for reimbursement in the following categories: • Meals: up to $60.00 per person × 3 = $180.00 • Hotel accommodation: Rate of day, 2 rooms (1 per adult), up to 3 nights, $500 maximum • Ground transportation: up to $200.00 (1-5 passengers) • Communication: up to $30.00 per person × 3 = $90.00 • Parking: up to $50.00 (1 day maximum) • Essential items: up to $30.00 per person × 3 = $90.00 Please note that all expenses must have been incurred during the disruption period and supported by valid receipts.
🤖
To process your claim, I'll need you to upload your receipts. Please upload photos or scans of your receipts for any expenses you incurred during the disruption.

• Receipt 4: Essential items — $55.00 CAD (toiletries + child items)
• Receipt 5: Communication — $45.00 CAD (phone calls + internet)

🤖
Great news! Based on the details of your flight, you are eligible for expense reimbursement. Your connecting flight AC 702 from Calgary to Vancouver was cancelled due to a mechanical safety issue, and you were stranded in Calgary for approximately 14 hours. Under the applicable regulations (APPR), you may be reimbursed for reasonable expenses incurred during this disruption.
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY
🤖
Based on the length of your disruption (14 hours), you may be eligible for reimbursement in the following categories: • Meals: up to $60.00 • Hotel accommodation: Rate of day (up to 3 nights, $500 maximum) • Ground transportation: up to $200.00 • Communication: up to $30.00 • Parking: up to $50.00 (1 day maximum) • Essential items: up to $30.00 Please note that all expenses must have been incurred during the disruption period and supported by valid receipts.
🤖
To process your claim, I'll need you to upload your receipts. Please upload photos or scans of your receipts for any expenses you incurred during the disruption.

• Receipt 4: Essential items — $28.00 CAD (toiletries)

PAYMENT VALIDATION:
📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify eligible when customer at mid-point, did not reach destination, disruption Safety.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: APPR
• Itinerary: Multi-segment
- Segment 1: AC 701 YYZ→YYC, Dep: 12-Jun 07:00 (operated normally)
- Segment 2: AC 702 YYC→YVR, Dep: 12-Jun 12:00 (cancelled — mechanical)
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 702 YYC→YVR (Segment 2 — the cancelled segment)
• Promised Itinerary:
- Seg 1: AC 701 YYZ→YYC, Dep: 12-Jun 07:00
- Seg 2: AC 702 YYC→YVR, Dep: 12-Jun 12:00
• Actual Itinerary: AC 701 completed. AC 702 cancelled (mechanical). Stranded at YYC. Rebooked next day.
• Departure Delay on SOC Segment: 840 min (14 hours)
 
• Departure delay code on SOC Segment: 44 (Spares and Maintenance Equipment — Safety)— Safety
• Controllability: Safety (Code 44)
• Passenger Type: Regular (Note: J cabin exception pax would skip controllability, but outcome is same — ELIGIBLE either way for Safety)
• Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Regular ✓ → 2. Controllability → Safety → ELIGIBLE
• PAX Count: 1 adult
• Class of Booking: J (Business)
• Revenue Booking: YES
• ACR Issued: Hotel: No, Transport: No, Meal: No
• Journey Completed: > 72h
• J CABIN MEAL NOTE: J cabin passengers receive meal entitlement at standard CCT rate ($60 for ). Business and economy currently have the same CCT meal allowance. If ACR meal voucher was issued, ACR amount would be deducted.
• ENTITLEMENTS (, 1 adult, J class, no ACR):
- Meals: $60/pax (J cabin — same CCT rate as economy currently)
- Hotel: Rate of day, 3 nights max, $500 cap
- Transport: $200 (1 pax)
- Communication: $30/pax
- Parking: $50, 1 day max
- Essentials: $30/pax

💬 Customer intent from test case
👤 I was on a connecting flight. My first leg went fine, but my second leg was cancelled because of a mechanical problem with the plane. I got stuck at my connection for about 14 hours. I had to pay for food, a hotel, and a taxi. Can I get reimbursed?

Great news! Based on the details of your flight, you are eligible for expense reimbursement. Your connecting flight AC 702 from Calgary to Vancouver was cancelled due to a mechanical Controllable issue, and you were stranded in Calgary for approximately 14 hours. Under the applicable regulations (APPR), you may be reimbursed for reasonable expenses incurred during this disruption.
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY

📋 Prerequisites / Description

Prerequisites:
• Regime: APPR
• Itinerary: Multi-segment
- Segment 1: AC 701 YYZ→YYC, Dep: 12-Jun 07:00 (operated normally)
- Segment 2: AC 702 YYC→YVR, Dep: 12-Jun 12:00 (cancelled — mechanical)
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 702 YYC→YVR (Segment 2)

- Seg 1: AC 701 YYZ→YYC, Dep: 12-Jun 07:00
- Seg 2: AC 702 YYC→YVR, Dep: 12-Jun 12:00
• Actual Itinerary: Incomplete journey. Stranded at mid-point (YYC).
• Departure Delay on SOC Segment: 840 min (14 hours)
 
• Departure Delay code: Not available
• Cancellation Code: TECH (Technical/Mechanical) — 
• Controllability: Controllable (via cancellation code TECH)

👤 My flight from Toronto to Vancouver was delayed by about 6 hours because of bad weather. I'm a Super Elite member and was travelling with a companion. We had to pay for meals, a hotel, and taxis. Can we get reimbursed?

💬 Test-case conversation from test case (Detailed Steps)
🤖
Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?
English
👤
🤖
Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice.
🤖
How can I help you today?
My flight from Toronto to Vancouver was delayed by about 6 hours because of bad weather. I'm a Super Elite member and was travelling with a companion. We had to pay for meals, a hotel, and taxis. Can we get reimbursed?
👤
⚙️ the chatbot identifies Standards of Care (SoC) intent
⚙️ the chatbot checks: Is this a third-party submission? → NO
🤖
Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited. Would you like to get started?
🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.
🤖
Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable.
[First Name] [Last Name]
👤
🤖
Thanks, [First Name]! And, what is your booking reference number?
PNR: [PNR Code]
👤

⚙️ Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND
🤖
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?
• Email: t***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***789
Email: t***@gmail.com
👤
🤖
I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it.
546879
👤
⚙️ the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
🤖
Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step.
⚙️ the chatbot checks if this PNR a group booking → No
⚙️ the chatbot checks if there are multiple PAX on PNR? → Yes (2 passengers)
🤖
How many people are on your claim?
🤖
I found 2 passengers on this booking: • [First Name] [Last Name] (Adult) — Super Elite 50K — Claimant • [Companion First Name] [Companion Last Name] (Adult) Is this who you'd like to include in the claim?
Yes, both
👤
⚙️ the chatbot checks: PAX count on PNR = 2 → ≤9 → NOT a group booking → PROCEED
🤖
I found the following journey: • Flight: AC 301 • Route: Toronto (YYZ) → Vancouver (YVR) • Date: June 13, 2026 • Departure Delay: 6 hours Is this the journey you'd like to check?
Yes
👤
⚙️ the chatbot checks if there are more than 1 segment → No
⚙️ the chatbot sends request to Case Services for duplicate check
⚙️ Case Services returns: NO existing case found for this passenger + journey combination
⚙️ the chatbot checks if it passed the expected Departure time of the last itinerary → Yes
⚙️ the chatbot checks has it been more than 72 hours for all segments in the selected journey → Yes
CLAIM TIME LIMIT CHECK
⚙️ the chatbot checks: Filing deadline for APPR SOC per province
⚙️ the system calculates:
• Origin: YYZ (Ontario) → Limitation = 730 days
• Destination: YVR (British Columbia) → Limitation = 1,095 days
• Days elapsed: Within limitation period → PROCEED
⚙️ the chatbot checks if there is triage data available? → Yes
TRIAGE & DDS EVALUATION
⚙️ the DDS evaluation is done in backend
⚙️ DDS Evaluates and sends to Trip Tracer UI:
TRIP TRACER UI VALIDATIONS
⚙️ the tester opens Trip Tracer UI for PNR
⚙️ ☐ Single-segment domestic trip YYZ→YVR on AC301 was delayed +6H 00M due to weather (uncontrollable — but IRRELEVANT for exception pax)
⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Flight: AC 301, Route: YYZ → YVR, Scheduled Dep: 12-Jun 08:00
⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Flight: AC 301, Route: YYZ → YVR, Status: ARRIVED LATE, Scheduled Dep: 08:00 → Actual: 14:00, Delay: +6H 00M
⚙️ ☐ CRITICAL: Exception pax → controllability SKIPPED. Regular pax + weather = NE-07. Exception pax + weather = EL-07.
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-APPR-EL-07
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
⚙️ the system sends case data to CyberSource for fraud screening (behind the scenes — not visible to passenger)
⚙️ CyberSource returns: GREEN (no fraud indicators detected)
⚙️ → PROCEED to eligible flow
🤖
Great news! Based on the details of your flight, both passengers on your booking are eligible for expense reimbursement. Your flight AC 301 from Toronto to Vancouver was delayed by approximately 6 hours. As a valued Super Elite member, you and your travel companion are eligible for reimbursement of reasonable expenses incurred during this disruption under the applicable regulations (APPR).
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY
🤖
Based on the length of your disruption (6 hours) and your party size (2 passengers), you may be eligible for reimbursement in the following categories: • Meals: up to $30.00 per person × 2 = $60.00 • Hotel accommodation: Rate of day, 2 rooms (1 per adult), up to 3 nights, $500 maximum • Ground transportation: up to $200.00 (1-5 passengers) • Communication: up to $30.00 per person × 2 = $60.00 • Parking: up to $50.00 (1 day maximum) • Essential items: up to $30.00 per person × 2 = $60.00 Please note that all expenses must have been incurred during the disruption period and supported by valid receipts.
🤖
To process your claim, I'll need you to upload your receipts. Please upload photos or scans of your receipts for any expenses you incurred during the disruption.
⚙️ the chatbot displays the receipt upload interface
receipt images:
👤
• Receipt 1: Meal receipt — $52.00 CAD (dinner for 2 at airport restaurant)
• Receipt 2: Communication — $40.00 CAD (Wi-Fi + phone calls for 2)
• Receipt 3: Essential items — $35.00 CAD (toiletries for 2)
⚙️ Overnight eligibility check: Delay >360 min AND ETD from 5 AM →
⚙️ Original ETD = 14:00 + 6h delay = new dep 20:00 → overnight may apply depending on Departure time.

PAYMENT VALIDATION:
📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify that Priority/Exception passengers (Aeroplan 100K, Star Gold, UMNR, elderly 70+, accessibility SSR, DIV, SIXF) are eligible regardless of disruption type under APPR.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: APPR
• Itinerary: Single segment — AC 301 YYZ→YVR
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 301 YYZ→YVR
• Promised Itinerary: AC 301 YYZ→YVR, Dep: 12-Jun 08:00
• Actual Itinerary: AC 301 YYZ→YVR, Dep: 12-Jun 14:00
• Departure Delay: 360 min (6 hours)
 
• Departure delay code: 77 (Weather) — Uncontrollable — BUT IRRELEVANT for exception pax
• Controllability: Uncontrollable (Code 77) — SKIPPED for exception pax
• Passenger Type: Exception — Super Elite 50K
• Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Super Elite 50K (EXCEPTION) → Controllability SKIPPED → ELIGIBLE
• Exception Privilege: Extends to ALL passengers on same PNR
• PAX Count: 2 adults (Super Elite 50K + companion)
• Class of Booking: J (Business)
• Revenue Booking: YES
• ACR Issued: Hotel: No, Transport: No, Meal: No
• Journey Completed: > 72h
• J CABIN MEAL NOTE: J cabin passengers receive meal entitlement at standard CCT rate ($30 for ). Business and economy currently have same CCT meal allowance.
• ENTITLEMENTS (, 2 adults, J class, no ACR):
- Meals: $30/pax × 2 = $60 (J cabin — same CCT rate as economy currently)
- Hotel: Rate of day, 2 rooms (1 per adult), 3 nights max, $500 cap (if overnight)
- Transport: $200 (1–5 pax)
- Communication: $30/pax × 2 = $60
- Parking: $50, 1 day max
- Essentials: $30/pax × 2 = $60

🤖
Great news! Based on the details of your flight, you are eligible for expense reimbursement. Your flight AC 301 from Toronto to Vancouver was delayed by approximately 7 hours due to a crew scheduling issue. Under the applicable regulations (APPR), you may be reimbursed for reasonable expenses incurred during this disruption.
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY
🤖
Based on the length of your disruption (7 hours), you may be eligible for reimbursement in the following categories: • Meals: up to $30.00 • Hotel accommodation: Rate of day (up to 3 nights, $500 maximum) • Ground transportation: up to $200.00 • Communication: up to $30.00 • Parking: up to $50.00 (1 day maximum) • Essential items: up to $30.00 Please note that all expenses must have been incurred during the disruption period and supported by valid receipts.
🤖
To process your claim, I'll need you to upload your receipts. Please upload photos or scans of your receipts for any expenses you incurred during the disruption.
⚙️ the chatbot displays the receipt upload interface
receipt images:
👤
• Receipt 1: Hotel receipt — $165.00 CAD (1 night)
• Receipt 2: Meal receipt — $25.00 CAD (dinner)
• Receipt 3: Taxi receipt — $50.00 CAD (airport to hotel + hotel to airport)
• Receipt 4: Communication — $15.00 CAD (Wi-Fi)
⚙️ RECEIPT READER — OCR VALIDATION (RR-01):
⚙️ the system processes uploaded receipts through OCR (Agentic Receipt Reader)
⚙️ the OCR engine extracts line items from each receipt:
• Line item: Hotel stay, 1 night — $165.00 CAD
• Date: June 14, 2026
• Category: Hotel
• Within disruption window: YES ✓
• Rate of day comparison: $165 vs rate of day → WITHIN range ✓
• Nights: 1 (within 3-night max) ✓
• Amount: $165 < $500 cap ✓
• OCR Result: PASS ✓
• Line item: Dinner — $25.00 CAD
• Date: June 14, 2026
• Category: Meals
• Within disruption window: YES ✓

💬 Customer intent from test case
👤 My Air Canada flight from Toronto to Halifax was delayed by about 4 hours because of an aircraft defect they found before takeoff. I had to buy food while waiting at the airport. Can I get reimbursed?

Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice.
🤖
How can I help you today?
My Air Canada flight from Toronto to Halifax was delayed by about 4 hours because of an aircraft defect they found before takeoff. I had to buy food while waiting at the airport. Can I get reimbursed?

To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited. Would you like to get started?
🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.

• Destination: YHZ (Nova Scotia) → Limitation = 730 days

TRIAGE & DDS EVALUATION

TRIP TRACER UI VALIDATIONS
⚙️ the tester opens Trip Tracer UI for PNR
⚙️ ☐ Single-segment domestic trip YYZ→YHZ on AC401 was delayed +4H 00M due to aircraft defect (safety)
⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Flight: AC 401, Route: YYZ → YHZ, Scheduled Dep: 16-Jun 09:00
⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Flight: AC 401, Route: YYZ → YHZ, Status: ARRIVED LATE, Scheduled Dep: 09:00 → Actual: 13:00, Delay: +4H 00M
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-APPR-EL-09
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
⚙️ the system sends case data to CyberSource for fraud screening (behind the scenes — not visible to passenger)
⚙️ CyberSource returns: GREEN (no fraud indicators detected)
⚙️ → PROCEED to eligible flow
🤖
Great news! Based on the details of your flight, you are eligible for expense reimbursement. Your flight AC 401 from Toronto to Halifax was delayed by approximately 4 hours due to an aircraft safety issue. Under the applicable regulations (APPR), you may be reimbursed for reasonable expenses incurred during this disruption.
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY
🤖
Based on the length of your disruption (4 hours), you may be eligible for reimbursement in the following categories: • Meals: up to $15.00 • Communication: up to $30.00 Please note that due to the length of the disruption (under 6 hours), hotel accommodation, ground transportation, parking, and essential items are not eligible for reimbursement at this time. All expenses must have been incurred during the disruption period and supported by valid receipts.
KEY DIFFERENCE FROM OTHER EL CASES
• Meals: $15 (NOT $30 or $60 — lower tier)
• Communication: $30 (>120 min ✓)
• Hotel: NOT ENTITLED ✗ (delay <360 min, no overnight)
• Transport: NOT ENTITLED ✗ (delay <360 min)
• Parking: NOT ENTITLED ✗ (delay <300 min)
• Essentials: NOT ENTITLED ✗ (delay <300 min)
🤖
To process your claim, I'll need you to upload your receipts. Please upload photos or scans of your receipts for any expenses you incurred during the disruption.
⚙️ the chatbot displays the receipt upload interface
receipt images:
👤
• Receipt 1: Meal receipt — $14.00 CAD (sandwich + coffee at airport)
• Receipt 2: Communication — $12.00 CAD (Wi-Fi access)

• Within disruption window: YES ✓
• Threshold check: $12 ≤ $30 (communication entitlement) ✓
• OCR Result: PASS ✓

PENDING RESULT
🤖
Thank you for submitting your claim details. Your flight AC 201 from Toronto to Montreal arrived recently, and we need a little more time to gather all the necessary flight data to evaluate your eligibility. Your claim is being processed and you will receive an update within the next few days.
🤖
Here's what will happen next: • We've created a reference for your claim: [Shell Case Reference] • Our system will automatically evaluate your eligibility once all flight data is available (typically within 72 hours of your last flight) • You will receive an email with a link to complete your claim if you are eligible • Please keep any receipts for expenses you incurred during the disruption, as you may need to upload them later
⚙️ the system's polling queue triggers a re-evaluation
• Actual departure time: confirmed
• Actual Departure time: confirmed
• Departure delay code: populated (e.g., 41 – Crew Scheduling)
• Operating data: complete
⚙️ the DDS evaluates eligibility with complete data:
• If ELIGIBLE: Deep link email sent to passenger → passenger clicks link → returns to chatbot → enters Welcome Back flow → uploads receipts → OCR validation → case creation → payment
• If NOT ELIGIBLE: Rejection email sent to passenger → case auto-closed
• If NO DETERMINATION: Case moved to manual agent queue
🤖
Good news! We've reviewed the details of your flight AC 201 from Toronto to Montreal, and you are eligible for expense reimbursement. Please click the link below to complete your claim by uploading your receipts.
• Deep link to chatbot with pre-populated claim data
• Case reference number
• Expiry date for completing the claim
⚙️ the chatbot opens with pre-populated data:
🤖
Welcome back, [First Name]! We've determined that you are eligible for expense reimbursement for your flight AC 201 from Toronto to Montreal on June 7, 2026. To complete your claim, please upload your receipts.
⚙️ the chatbot proceeds to:
• Receipt upload flow
• OCR validation
• Expense breakdown
• Case creation
• Payment flow
END FLOW (GLOB-11)

⚙️ the system processing queue is visible to management only (not agents)
⚙️ agents cannot see or action cases in system processing queue
⚙️ System Processing (Pending — 72-hour hold)
⚙️ the case is NOT in read-only mode (will be updated after DDS runs post-72h)
PAYMENT VALIDATION
⚙️ a shell case is created in System Processing Queue
⚙️ the route is AC 201 YYZ → YVR with delay 360 min (6 hrs) filed less than 72 hrs since Departure
⚙️ the system polls DDS for up to 14 days
⚙️ after 72h if DDS returns ELIGIBLE then a deep link email is sent to customer
⚙️ the customer returns to chatbot and uploads receipts for OCR validation
⚙️ after 72h if DDS returns NOT ELIGIBLE then rejection email is sent to passenger
⚙️ if no DDS answer after 14 days then the case is sent to agent queue for manual handling
⚙️ Pending / System Processing
EXPECTED RESULTS
⚙️ SoC-APPR-PE-01. PENDING. Last leg <72h ago. Shell case created. Placed in polling queue. Passenger informed of pending status. After 72h window: RE retrigger → DDS evaluation → deep link sent if eligible. No receipt upload at this stage.

📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify that when the customer's last leg landed < 72 hours ago, the system applies the 72-hour hold, creates a shell case, and sends the customer away with email confirmation.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: APPR
• Itinerary: Single segment — AC 201 YYZ→YUL
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 201 YYZ→YUL
• Promised Itinerary: AC 201 YYZ→YUL, Dep: [today-1] 08:00
• Actual Itinerary: AC 201 YYZ→YUL, Dep: [today-1] 14:00
• Departure Delay: 360 min (6 hours)
 
• Controllability: TBD – within 72-hour window
• Departure delay code: TBD – pending RE evaluation
• Passenger Type: Regular
• Check Order: 72-hour check → FAIL (< 72h) → PENDING
• PAX Count: 1 adult / Class: PY / Revenue: YES / Journey: < 72h

Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?
English

Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice.
🤖
How can I help you today?
My flight from London to Toronto was delayed by about 6 hours and I had to buy food and pay for a hotel overnight. Can I get reimbursed for what I spent?
👤

⚙️ the chatbot checks: Is this a third-party submission? → NO
🤖
Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited. Would you like to get started?
🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.
🤖
Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable.

⚙️ ☐ Single-segment trip LHR→YYZ on AC849 was delayed +6H 00M

🤖
Air Canada employees are not eligible for claims under CCT, but are eligible for bag use cases, as decided by Mohammed.

Employees receive a 'not eligible' response if they attempt to claim under CCT.

🤖
I've reviewed the details of your booking. Unfortunately, your booking is classified as an employee booking, which does not qualify for Standards of Care expense reimbursement under the applicable regulations. If you incurred expenses during this disruption, please contact your employee services team for guidance on available options.
🤖
Do you agree with this assessment, or would you like to dispute it?
• Agree
• Dispute
Agree
👤

🤖
Dispute
⚙️ → Case created with dispute flag
⚙️ → Case sent to manual handling queue
⚙️ → Agent reviews employee status and circumstances
⚙️ → Employee remains not eligible for CCT but may be directed to

PAYMENT VALIDATION:
📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify NOT ELIGIBLE for AC employee booking under EU regime.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: EU
• Itinerary: Single segment — AC 849 LHR→YYZ
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 849 LHR→YYZ
• Promised Itinerary: AC 849 LHR→YYZ, Dep: 01-Jun 19:00
• Actual Itinerary: AC 849 LHR→YYZ, Dep: 02-Jun 01:00
• Departure Delay: 360 min (6 hours)
 
• Departure delay code: 41 (Aircraft Defects / Maintenance Finding) — N/A (employee not eligible regardless)
• Passenger Type: Employee (AC) – office suffix ES/OT/EP/EE/EC
• Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Employee → NOT ELIGIBLE (no further checks)
• PAX Count: 1 adult / Class: PY / Revenue: NO – Employee / Journey: > 72h
• Employee Flow: NOT blocked upfront. Goes through OTP first, then receives "Not Eligible."
• Policy: Not eligible under CCT (decided by Mohammed). Eligible for baggage claims only.

• Regime: EU
• Itinerary: Single segment — AC 849 LHR→YYZ
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 849 LHR→YYZ
• Promised Itinerary: AC 849 LHR→YYZ, Dep: 01-Jun 19:00
• Actual Itinerary: AC 849 LHR→YYZ, Dep: 02-Jun 01:00
• Departure Delay: 360 min (6 hours)
 
• Departure delay code: 41 (Aircraft Defects / Maintenance Finding) — N/A (employee not eligible regardless)
• Passenger Type: Employee (AC) – office suffix ES/OT/EP/EE/EC
• Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Employee → NOT ELIGIBLE (no further checks)
• PAX Count: 1 adult / Class: PY / Revenue: NO – Employee / Journey: > 72h
• Employee Flow: NOT blocked upfront. Goes through OTP first, then receives "Not Eligible."
• Policy: Not eligible under CCT (decided by Mohammed). Eligible for baggage claims only.

⚙️ the tester opens Trip Tracer UI for PNR
⚙️ ☐ Single-segment trip CDG→YUL on AC871 was delayed +18H 00M — passenger notified 18 days before departure
⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Flight: AC 871, Route: CDG → YUL, Scheduled Dep: 20-Jun 11:00
⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Flight: AC 871, Route: CDG → YUL, Status: ARRIVED LATE, Scheduled Dep: 11:00 → Actual Dep: 21-Jun 08:00, Delay: +18H 00M (note: actual is +21h per times, but prereqs say 18h / 1080 min)
⚙️ ☐ Notification: 18 days before departure → ≥14 days threshold → NOT ELIGIBLE
• Passenger type check:
⚙️ - Regular → proceed to notification check
• Notification check:
⚙️ - 18 days > 14 days → PASSENGER WAS NOTIFIED IN ADVANCE → NOT ELIGIBLE
CRITICAL REGIME DIFFERENCE
⚙️ - Under APPR, 18 days advance notice → would still be NE (exceeds 12h)
⚙️ - Under EU, 18 days advance notice → NE (exceeds 14 days)
• All further checks SKIPPED because notification threshold exceeded:
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-EU-NE-03
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
🤖
I've reviewed the details of your flight. Our records show that you were notified about the schedule change for flight AC 871 from Paris to Montreal approximately 18 days before your scheduled departure. Under the applicable EU regulations, expense reimbursement is not available when passengers are notified of the disruption at least 14 days before the scheduled departure date, as this provides sufficient time to make alternative arrangements.
🤖

📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify NOT ELIGIBLE when customer informed ≥14 days before departure under EU.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: EU
• Itinerary: Single segment — AC 871 CDG→YUL
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 871 CDG→YUL
• Promised Itinerary: AC 871 CDG→YUL, Dep: 20-Jun 11:00
• Actual Itinerary: AC 871 CDG→YUL, Dep: 21-Jun 08:00
• Departure delay code: 64 (Flight Crew Shortage) — Classification: Controllable (irrelevant — notification check takes precedence; EU doesn't check controllability for SOC anyway)
• Departure delay: 1080 min (18 hours) 
• EU Notification Threshold: ≥14 days (NOT 14 days)
• 18 days ≥ 14 days → NOT ELIGIBLE
• Regime: EU (CDG departure)
• Filing deadline: France → 1,825 days (5 years)
• CRITICAL REGIME DIFFERENCE:
APPR: 12 HOURS notification threshold
EU: 14 DAYS notification threshold
ASL: 14 DAYS notification threshold

• 18 days ≥ 14 days → NOT ELIGIBLE

• Filing deadline: France → 1,825 days (5 years)

APPR: 12 HOURS notification threshold
EU: 14 DAYS notification threshold
ASL: 14 DAYS notification threshold

Unfortunately, under the applicable EU regulations, expense reimbursement is only available for delays of 2 hours or more. Since your delay was below this threshold, your claim is not eligible for reimbursement.

💬 Customer intent from test case
👤 My flight from Toronto to London was delayed by about 8 hours. I had to buy food and pay for a hotel overnight at the airport. Can I get reimbursed?
🗺️ Canonical Miro path — coverage (Standards of Care / SoC)
✓ asserted · ✕ required-by-Miro but missing · · not applicable
✓ GLOB-01✓ —✓ GenUC-23· GLOB-19 · ADD-112✓ GenUC-24✕ SOC-01✓ GenUC-01✓ GenUC-05✓ ADD-99· GenUC-06· GenUC-07✓ GenUC-08✓ GenUC-18✓ GenUC-10✓ SoC-02a · ADD-98✓ SoC-02a · GenUC-25 · ADD-83✓ ADD-105✓ SoC-02b✕ SOC-03c· SOC-03d✕ SOC-03e· SOC-03f✓ SOC-03g✓ SoC-03b · ADD-23 · GLOB-05✓ RR-01✓ SoC-03b✓ SoC-03b · ADD-27/106✓ GenUC-15✓ GenUC-16/19✕ GenUC-27✓ GLOB-11
⚠️ Gap analysis vs Miro · 1 low
LOWWeather / uncontrollable NE: Miro wording states the delay was outside the airline's control and is not covered under the regulation (ADD-27/ADD-106). Not asserted.

I've reviewed the details of your flight. Your flight AC 848 departed from Toronto (Canada), which is outside the European Union. Under EU Regulation 261/2004, expense reimbursement is only available for flights departing from an EU/EEA or UK airport when operated by a non-EU carrier. Since your flight did not depart from within the EU, your claim is not eligible under EU regulations. However, your flight may be eligible under Canadian regulations (APPR). Would you like us to check your eligibility under APPR instead?
🤖

📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify NOT ELIGIBLE when itinerary origin is outside EU.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: APPR
• Itinerary: Single segment — AC 848 YYZ→LHR
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 848 YYZ→LHR
• Promised Itinerary: AC 848 YYZ→LHR, Dep: 05-Jun 19:00
• Actual Itinerary: AC 848 YYZ→LHR, Dep: 06-Jun 01:00
• Departure Delay: 360 min (6 hours) / Delay Category: 
• Departure delay code: 64 (Crew Shortage) — Controllable
• Passenger Type: Regular
• Check Order: 1. Geographic origin check → YYZ (Canada) → NOT in EU → NOT ELIGIBLE under EU
• CRITICAL REGIME DIFFERENCES — GEOGRAPHIC CHECKS:
- APPR: NO geographic origin check — applies to all Canadian airline flights
- EU: Flight must DEPART FROM EU/EEA/UK airport (origin check ONLY)
- ASL: Flight must involve Israel (to/from TLV, SDV, ETH, EIY)
• NOTE: This itinerary WOULD be eligible under APPR (Canadian departure, AC flight, controllable delay ≥2h). System should route to APPR instead.
• PAX Count: 1 adult / Class: PY / Revenue: YES / Journey: > 72h

• Itinerary: Single segment — AC 848 YYZ→LHR

- EU: Flight must DEPART FROM EU/EEA/UK airport (origin check ONLY)
- ASL: Flight must involve Israel (to/from TLV, SDV, ETH, EIY)

• PAX Count: 1 adult / Class: PY / Revenue: YES / Journey: > 72h

⚙️ ☐ Single-segment trip YUL→LHR on AC855 was delayed +6H 00M — origin NOT in UK (YUL = Canada)

⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Flight: AC 855, Route: YUL → LHR, Scheduled Dep: 06-Jun 20:00

WHY SEPARATE FROM NE-06
🤖
Retained EU Law
⚙️ The system needs to check BOTH:
⚙️ - NE-06: Did the flight start in the EU/EEA? → NO
⚙️ - NE-07: Did the flight start in the UK? → NO
⚙️ In this case, YUL→LHR fails BOTH gates because Canada is neither EU nor UK.
• All further checks SKIPPED:
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-EU-NE-07
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
🤖
I've reviewed the details of your flight. Your flight AC 855 departed from Montreal (Canada), which is outside the United Kingdom. Under UK aviation regulations (retained EU261), expense reimbursement is only available for flights departing from a UK airport when operated by a non-UK carrier. Since your flight did not depart from the UK, your claim is not eligible under UK regulations. However, your flight may be eligible under Canadian regulations (APPR). Would you like us to check your eligibility under APPR instead?
🤖

• Controllability: Controllable (Code 64) — NOT EVALUATED (geographic check fails first; EU doesn't check controllability for SOC anyway)

• Note: UK regime requires itinerary to originate from UK. YUL→LHR starts in Canada.

🤖
Unfortunately, too much time has passed since your flight disruption to submit this claim. Your flight AC 835 from Brussels to Toronto was disrupted on January 15, 2025 — approximately 509 days ago. Under the applicable EU regulations for flights departing from Belgium, the filing deadline is 365 days (1 year) from the date of disruption. Since this deadline has passed, your claim is no longer eligible for expense reimbursement.

• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 835 BRU→YYZ
• Promised Itinerary: AC 835 BRU→YYZ, Dep: 15-Jan-2025 10:00
• Actual Itinerary: AC 835 BRU→YYZ, Dep: 15-Jan-2025 17:00
• Departure Delay: 420 min (7 hours)
 
• Departure delay code: 64 (Flight Crew Shortage) — Controllable (present in flight data but never evaluated — limitation check fails first)
• Controllability: Controllable (Code 64) — NOT EVALUATED (limitation expired)
• Passenger Type: Regular
• Check Order: 1. Limitation period check → EXPIRED → NOT ELIGIBLE
• Claim Filing Date: Beyond EU limitation period
• Limitation Period: Belgium = 365 days (1 year)
• Disruption Date: January 15, 2025
• Filing Date: June 8, 2026
• Days Elapsed: 509 days → 509 > 365 → EXPIRED
• PAX Count: 1 adult / Class: PY / Revenue: YES / Journey: > 72h (well beyond)

My flight from London to Toronto was cancelled and I was rebooked on a flight about 2 and a half hours later. I had to buy food while waiting at Heathrow. Can I get reimbursed?

Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited. Would you like to get started?
🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.

⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Flight: AC 849, Route: LHR → YYZ, Status: CANCELLED — Rebooked, SOC Segment Departure Delay: +1H 30M
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-EU-NE-09
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
🤖
I've reviewed the details of your flight. Your flight AC 849 from London to Toronto was cancelled and you were rebooked on a later flight. However, the total delay to your journey was 2 hours and 30 minutes, which is below the minimum 2-hour threshold required for expense reimbursement under the applicable EU regulations for passengers who did not travel on their original flight.
🤖

• Agree
• Dispute
Agree
👤

I've reviewed the details of your flight. Your connecting flight AC 847 from Frankfurt to Toronto was cancelled and you were rebooked on a later flight. However, the total delay to your journey was 2 hours and 50 minutes, which is below the minimum 2-hour threshold required for expense reimbursement under the applicable EU regulations for passengers whose journey was incomplete.

Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?
English

Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice.
🤖
How can I help you today?
I didn't end up taking my flight from London to Toronto because I heard there were issues. The flight apparently still operated though. I had already arranged alternative travel and incurred some costs. Can I get reimbursed?
👤
⚙️ the chatbot identifies Standards of Care (SoC) intent
⚙️ the chatbot checks: Is this a third-party submission? → NO
🤖
Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited. Would you like to get started?
🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.
🤖
Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable.
[First Name] [Last Name]

• Phone: +1***789

CLAIM TIME LIMIT CHECK
⚙️ the chatbot checks: Filing deadline for EU SOC per departing country
⚙️ the system calculates:

• Days elapsed: 2 days → Within limitation period → PROCEED

TRIAGE & DDS EVALUATION

⚙️ DDS Evaluates and sends to Trip Tracer UI:
TRIP TRACER UI VALIDATIONS
⚙️ the tester opens Trip Tracer UI for PNR
⚙️ ☐ Single-segment trip LHR→YYZ on AC849 — flight operated as scheduled. Passenger chose not to board.
⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Flight: AC 849, Route: LHR → YYZ, Scheduled Dep: 09-Jun 10:00
⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Flight: AC 849, Route: LHR → YYZ, Status: OPERATED — On time (passenger did not board)
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-EU-NE-12
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
🤖
I've reviewed the details of your flight. Our records show that flight AC 849 from London to Toronto operated as scheduled and was not cancelled. Since the flight was not disrupted, expense reimbursement is not available under the applicable EU regulations. If you chose not to travel for personal reasons, you may want to review your ticket's fare rules for refund or rebooking options.

⚙️ I've reviewed the details of your flight. Our records show that flight AC 871 from Paris to Toronto operated and was not recorded as cancelled. Since the flight was not recorded as a cancellation in our system, expense reimbursement is not available under the applicable EU regulations for this journey. If you believe this assessment is incorrect — for example, if your flight was diverted back and you never reached your destination — you may dispute this decision and our team will review your case manually.

⚙️ → Agent investigates:
⚙️ → Agent can override to ELIGIBLE if diversion qualifies as cancellation

💬 Customer intent from test case
👤 I was on a connecting flight from London through Frankfurt to Toronto. My first leg was fine, but I got stuck in Frankfurt. The airline says my connecting flight from Frankfurt to Toronto wasn't cancelled — it just left without me because I arrived late from the first flight. I had to buy meals and stay overnight. Can I get reimbursed?

I've reviewed the details of your flight. Our records show that your connecting flight AC 847 from Frankfurt to Toronto operated and was not cancelled. Since the connecting flight was not recorded as a cancellation, expense reimbursement is not available under the applicable EU regulations for this journey. If you believe you missed your connection due to a delay on a previous Air Canada flight, you may dispute this decision and our team will review your case manually.

 No travel
• Departure delay code: N/A – flight operated normally
• Controllability: N/A – SOC segment not cancelled; EU does not evaluate controllability for SOC anyway

How can I help you today?
I had a connecting flight from London to Munich through Frankfurt. The Air Canada leg from London to Frankfurt was fine, but the Lufthansa leg from Frankfurt to Munich was delayed by about 5 hours. I had to buy food and pay for a hotel. Can I get reimbursed?

I've reviewed the details of your journey. The disruption on your trip was caused by a delay on your United Airlines flight UA 8765 from Chicago to San Francisco, which was operated by a Star Alliance partner. Since this segment was not operated by Air Canada, we are unable to determine your eligibility for expense reimbursement automatically at this time. We have created a case for our team to review. In the meantime, please have any receipts for expenses you incurred during the disruption ready, as our team may request them during the review.

• Controllability: Unknown – STAR disruption data not fully available to AC; EU doesn't check controllability for SOC anyway
• Passenger Type: Regular
• Check Order: 1. SOC segment carrier check → STAR → No Determination
• PAX Count: 1 adult / Class: PY / Revenue: YES / Journey: > 72h

Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?
English

Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice.
🤖
How can I help you today?
My Air Canada flight from London to Toronto was delayed by about 7 hours. I had to buy food, pay for a hotel overnight, and take a taxi. Can I get reimbursed for what I spent?
👤

Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited. Would you like to get started?
🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.

CLAIM TIME LIMIT CHECK
⚙️ the chatbot checks: Filing deadline for EU SOC per departing country
⚙️ the system calculates:
• Departing country: United Kingdom → Limitation = 2,190 days (6 years)
• Days elapsed: Within limitation period → PROCEED

⚙️ the tester opens Trip Tracer UI for PNR
⚙️ ☐ Single-segment trip LHR→YYZ on AC848 was delayed +7H 00M due to weather (uncontrollable — NOT CHECKED under EU)
⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Flight: AC 848, Route: LHR → YYZ, Scheduled Dep: 18-Jun 11:00
⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Flight: AC 848, Route: LHR → YYZ, Status: ARRIVED LATE, Scheduled Dep: 11:00 → Actual: 18:00, Delay: +7H 00M
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-EU-EL-01

⚙️ → PROCEED to eligible flow
🤖
Great news! Based on the details of your flight, you are eligible for expense reimbursement. Your flight AC 848 from London to Toronto was delayed by approximately 7 hours. Under the applicable EU regulations (EC 261/2004), you may be reimbursed for reasonable expenses incurred during this disruption.
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY
🤖
Based on the length of your disruption (7 hours), you may be eligible for reimbursement in the following categories: • Meals: up to €30.00 • Hotel accommodation: Rate of day (up to 3 nights, €500 maximum) • Ground transportation: up to €200.00 • Communication: up to €30.00 • Parking: up to €50.00 (1 day maximum) • Essential items: up to €30.00
EU CURRENCY NOTE
⚙️ 1. Receipt currency → regime currency (EUR/GBP) for threshold comparison
⚙️ 2. Regime currency → country of residence currency for payout display
🤖
RECEIPT UPLOAD — WELCOME BACK FLOW:
🤖
To process your claim, I'll need you to upload your receipts. Please upload photos or scans of your receipts for any expenses you incurred during the disruption.
⚙️ the chatbot displays the receipt upload interface
receipt images:
👤
• Receipt 1: Hotel receipt — £145.00 GBP (1 night near Heathrow)
• Receipt 2: Meal receipt — £38.00 GBP (dinner + breakfast)
• Receipt 3: Taxi receipt — £42.00 GBP (airport to hotel + hotel to airport)
• Receipt 4: Communication — £12.00 GBP (Wi-Fi + phone calls)
⚙️ RECEIPT READER — OCR VALIDATION (RR-01):
⚙️ the system processes uploaded receipts through OCR (Agentic Receipt Reader)
⚙️ the OCR engine extracts line items from each receipt:
• Line item: Hotel stay, 1 night — £145.00 GBP
• Date: June 10, 2026
• Category: Hotel
• Within disruption window: YES ✓
• Currency conversion: £145 GBP → ~€170 EUR (for threshold comparison)
• Rate of day comparison: €170 vs rate of day (Heathrow area) → WITHIN range ✓
• Nights: 1 (within 3-night max) ✓
• Amount: €170 < €500 cap ✓
• OCR Result: PASS ✓
• Line item 1: Dinner — £22.00 GBP
• Line item 2: Breakfast — £16.00 GBP
• Total meals: £38.00 GBP → ~€45 EUR
• Date: June 10-11, 2026
• Category: Meals
• Within disruption window: YES ✓
• Threshold check: €45 ≤ €30 → FLAGGED — exceeds €30 meal threshold
• OCR Result: FLAGGED FOR AGENT REVIEW
• Line item 1: Taxi airport→hotel — £24.00 GBP
• Line item 2: Taxi hotel→airport — £18.00 GBP
• Total transport: £42.00 GBP → ~€49 EUR
• Date: June 10-11, 2026
• Category: Transport
• Within disruption window: YES ✓
• Threshold check: €49 ≤ €200 ✓
• OCR Result: PASS ✓
• Line item 1: Wi-Fi — £7.00 GBP
• Line item 2: Phone calls — £5.00 GBP
• Total communication: £12.00 GBP → ~€14 EUR
• Date: June 10, 2026
• Category: Communication
• Within disruption window: YES ✓
• Threshold check: €14 ≤ €30 ✓
• OCR Result: PASS ✓
RECEIPT VALIDATION SUMMARY
⚙️ the system displays the OCR validation summary:
• Total receipts processed: 4
• Total line items extracted: 6
• Items PASSED: 5
• Items FLAGGED for agent review: 1 (meals exceed €30 threshold)
• Items FAILED: 0
AGENT REVIEW REQUIRED
• Option A: Approve up to €30, reject €15 overage → Approved meals = €30
• Option B: Approve full amount with justification (e.g., airport pricing)
• Option C: Reject entire meal receipt and request resubmission
• Hotel: £145.00 / €170.00 ✓
• Meals: £25.50 / €30.00 ✓ (capped at €30 threshold)
• Transport: £42.00 / €49.00 ✓
• Communication: £12.00 / €14.00 ✓
• Parking: £0.00 / €0.00 (no receipt submitted)
• Essentials: £0.00 / €0.00 (no receipt submitted)
• TOTAL APPROVED: €263.00 EUR (displayed in country of residence currency for payout)
CASE CREATION (GLOB-09)
⚙️ the system creates a case in Case Services
• DDS response code: SoC-EU-EL-01
• Eligibility: ELIGIBLE
• Regime: EU (EC 261/2004)
• Departure country: United Kingdom
• Currency: GBP (receipts) → EUR (threshold comparison) → [Country of residence currency] (payout)
• All approved line items with amounts
• Flagged item: Meals capped at €30 (excess €15 rejected)
• Receipt images attached
• Fraud screening result: GREEN
• Note: Weather disruption (Code 77) — would be NOT ELIGIBLE under APPR but ELIGIBLE under EU (no controllability check)
🤖

Here's a summary: • Case Reference: [Case Number] • Total claim amount: €263.00 EUR • Categories: Hotel (€170.00), Meals (€30.00 — capped at threshold), Transport (€49.00), Communication (€14.00) • Note: Your meal expenses exceeded the maximum threshold of €30.00. The approved amount has been adjusted accordingly. You will receive a confirmation email shortly with your case details and next steps.

📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify ELIGIBLE when customer stayed at origin,  delay ≥2h, EU regime. Full eligible flow through receipt upload and case creation.

• Regime: EU 
•  Carrier: AC (AC 848 LHR→YYZ) 
• Promised Itinerary: AC 848 LHR→YYZ, Dep: 18-Jun 11:00, Arr: 18-Jun 15:00 
• Actual Itinerary: Flight cancelled. Rebooked next day. Passenger stayed at origin (LHR). 
• Delay Length: 420 min (7 hours)   
• Controllability: NOT CHECKED – EU does not evaluate controllability for SOC 
• Departure delay code: 77 (Weather – IRRELEVANT under EU SOC) 
• Passenger Type: Regular 
• Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Regular ✓ → 2. No-travel delay check → ≥2h → ELIGIBLE (no controllability check) 
• PAX Count: 1 adult 
• Class of Booking: PY 
• Revenue Booking: YES 
• ACR Issued: Hotel: No, Transport: No, Meal: No 
• Journey Completed: > 72h ENTITLEMENTS (, 1 adult, PY, no ACR): 
• Meals: $30/pax (converted to EUR for EU regime) 
• Hotel: Rate of day, max 3 nights, $500 cap 
• Transport: $200 (1-5 pax) 
• Communication: $30/pax 
• Parking: $50, 1 day max 
• Essentials: $30/pax

Great news! Based on the details of your flight, you are eligible for expense reimbursement. Your flight AC 871 from Paris to Toronto was cancelled and you were unable to travel for approximately 18 hours. Under the applicable EU regulations (EC 261/2004), you may be reimbursed for reasonable expenses incurred during this disruption.
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY
🤖
Based on the length of your disruption (18 hours), you may be eligible for reimbursement in the following categories: • Meals: up to €60.00 • Hotel accommodation: Rate of day (up to 3 nights, €500 maximum) • Ground transportation: up to €200.00 • Communication: up to €30.00 • Parking: up to €50.00 (1 day maximum) • Essential items: up to €30.00 Please note that all expenses must have been incurred during the disruption period and supported by valid receipts.

👤 My flight from Frankfurt to Toronto took off but had to turn around and come back to Frankfurt because of an engine issue. I was stuck at the airport for 14 hours and had to pay for food, a hotel, and a taxi. Can I get reimbursed?
