🤖
Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?
English
👤
🤖
Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice.
🤖
How can I help you today?
My flight from Frankfurt to Toronto took off but had to turn around and come back to Frankfurt because of an engine issue. I was stuck at the airport for 14 hours and had to pay for food, a hotel, and a taxi. Can I get reimbursed?
👤
⚙️ the chatbot identifies Standards of Care (SoC) intent
⚙️ the chatbot checks: Is this a third-party submission? → NO
🤖
Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited. Would you like to get started?
🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.
🤖
Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable.
[First Name] [Last Name]
👤
🤖
Thanks, [First Name]! And, what is your booking reference number?
PNR: [PNR Code]
👤

• Email: t***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***789
Email: t***@gmail.com

🤖
I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it.

I found the following journey: • Flight: AC 837 • Route: Frankfurt (FRA) → Toronto (YYZ) • Date: June 12, 2026 • Status: DIVERTED – Returned to origin (FRA) Is this the journey you'd like to check?
Yes
👤
⚙️ the chatbot checks if there are more than 1 segment → No
⚙️ the chatbot sends request to Case Services for duplicate check
⚙️ Case Services returns: NO existing case found for this passenger + journey combination
⚙️ the chatbot checks if it passed the expected Departure time of the last itinerary → Yes
⚙️ the chatbot checks has it been more than 72 hours for all segments in the selected journey → Yes
CLAIM TIME LIMIT CHECK
⚙️ the chatbot checks: Filing deadline for EU SOC per departing country
⚙️ the system calculates:
• Departing country: Germany → Limitation = 1,460 days (4 years)
• Days elapsed: Within limitation period → PROCEED
⚙️ the chatbot checks if there is triage data available? → Yes
TRIAGE & DDS EVALUATION
⚙️ the DDS evaluation is done in backend
⚙️ DDS Evaluates and sends to Trip Tracer UI:
TRIP TRACER UI VALIDATIONS
⚙️ the tester opens Trip Tracer UI for PNR
⚙️ ☐ Single-segment trip FRA→YYZ on AC837 was diverted — returned to origin (FRA) due to mechanical issue. Passenger returned to FRA, rebooked 14 hours later.
⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Flight: AC 837, Route: FRA → YYZ, Scheduled Dep: 12-Jun (time per prereqs)
⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Flight: AC 837, Route: FRA → YYZ, Status: DIVERTED — Returned to origin (FRA)
⚙️ ☐ Diversion treated as cancellation ✓ | SOC Segment Delay: 840 min (14h) → ≥2h ✓
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-EU-EL-03
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
⚙️ the system sends case data to CyberSource for fraud screening (behind the scenes)
⚙️ CyberSource returns: GREEN (no fraud indicators detected)
⚙️ → PROCEED to eligible flow
🤖
Great news! Based on the details of your flight, you are eligible for expense reimbursement. Your flight AC 837 from Frankfurt to Toronto was diverted back to Frankfurt due to a mechanical issue, and you were unable to travel for approximately 14 hours. Under the applicable EU regulations (EC 261/2004), you may be reimbursed for reasonable expenses incurred during this disruption.
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY
🤖
Based on the length of your disruption (14 hours), you may be eligible for reimbursement in the following categories: • Meals: up to €60.00 • Hotel accommodation: Rate of day (up to 3 nights, €500 maximum) • Ground transportation: up to €200.00 • Communication: up to €30.00 • Parking: up to €50.00 (1 day maximum) • Essential items: up to €30.00 Please note that all expenses must have been incurred during the disruption period and supported by valid receipts.
🤖
To process your claim, I'll need you to upload your receipts. Please upload photos or scans of your receipts for any expenses you incurred during the disruption.
⚙️ the chatbot displays the receipt upload interface
receipt images:
👤
• Receipt 1: Hotel receipt — €160.00 EUR (1 night near Frankfurt airport)
• Receipt 2: Meal receipt — €48.00 EUR (dinner + breakfast)
• Receipt 3: Taxi receipt — €55.00 EUR (airport to hotel + hotel to airport)
⚙️ RECEIPT READER — OCR VALIDATION (RR-01):
⚙️ the system processes uploaded receipts through OCR (Agentic Receipt Reader)
⚙️ the OCR engine extracts line items from each receipt:
• Line item: Hotel stay, 1 night — €160.00 EUR
• Date: June 12, 2026
• Category: Hotel
• Within disruption window: YES ✓
• Rate of day comparison: €160 vs rate of day (Frankfurt) → WITHIN range ✓
• Nights: 1 ✓ | Amount: €160 < €500 cap ✓
• OCR Result: PASS ✓
• Line item 1: Dinner — €28.00 EUR
• Line item 2: Breakfast — €20.00 EUR
• Total meals: €48.00 EUR
• Within disruption window: YES ✓
• Threshold check: €48 ≤ €60 ✓
• OCR Result: PASS ✓
• Line item 1: Taxi airport→hotel — €30.00 EUR
• Line item 2: Taxi hotel→airport — €25.00 EUR
• Total transport: €55.00 EUR
• Within disruption window: YES ✓
• Threshold check: €55 ≤ €200 ✓
• OCR Result: PASS ✓
RECEIPT VALIDATION SUMMARY
• Total receipts processed: 3
• Total line items extracted: 5
• Items PASSED: 5 | Items FLAGGED: 0 | Items FAILED: 0
EXPENSE BREAKDOWN
• Hotel: €160.00 ✓
• Meals: €48.00 ✓
• Transport: €55.00 ✓
• Communication: €0.00 | Parking: €0.00 | Essentials: €0.00
• TOTAL APPROVED: €263.00 EUR
CASE CREATION (GLOB-09)
⚙️ the system creates a case in Case Services
🤖
Your claim has been submitted successfully. Here's a summary: • Case Reference: [Case Number] • Total claim amount: €263.00 EUR • Categories: Hotel (€160.00), Meals (€48.00), Transport (€55.00) You will receive a confirmation email shortly.
⚙️ the system triggers eligible confirmation email

PAYMENT VALIDATION:
📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify ELIGIBLE when customer at mid-point, didn't reach destination,  delay ≥2h, EU.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: EU
• Itinerary: Multi-segment
- Segment 1: AC 852 LHR→YYZ (operated normally)
- Segment 2: AC 853 YYZ→YVR (cancelled)
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 853 YYZ→YVR (Segment 2 — cancelled)
• Promised Itinerary: AC 852 LHR→YYZ, then AC 853 YYZ→YVR
• Actual Itinerary: AC 852 completed. AC 853 cancelled. Stranded at YYZ. Rebooked 10 hours later.
• Departure Delay on SOC Segment: 600 min (10 hours)
 
• Departure delay code: 64 (Flight Crew Shortage) — Controllable — NOT CHECKED under EU SOC (EU does not evaluate controllability for SOC; code present in flight data but irrelevant to eligibility determination)
• Controllability: NOT CHECKED – EU regime does not evaluate controllability for SOC
• Passenger Type: Regular
• Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Regular ✓ → 2. No-travel departure delay check → ≥2h → ELIGIBLE (no controllability check)
• PAX Count: 1 adult
• Class of Booking: J (Business)
• Revenue Booking: YES
• ACR Issued: Hotel: No, Transport: No, Meal: No
• Journey Completed: > 72h
• J CABIN MEAL NOTE: J cabin passengers receive meal entitlement at standard CCT rate ($30 for ). Business and economy currently have same CCT meal allowance.
• ENTITLEMENTS (, 1 adult, J class, no ACR):
- Meals: $30/pax (J class — same CCT rate as economy currently)
- Hotel: Rate of day, 3 nights max, $500 cap (if overnight)
- Transport: $200 (1 pax)
- Communication: $30/pax
- Parking: $50, 1 day max
- Essentials: $30/pax

• Departure delay code: 64 (Flight Crew Shortage) — Controllable — NOT CHECKED under EU SOC (EU does not evaluate controllability for SOC; code present in flight data but irrelevant to eligibility determination)

• ENTITLEMENTS (, 1 adult, J class, no ACR):
- Meals: $30/pax (J class — same CCT rate as economy currently)
- Hotel: Rate of day, 3 nights max, $500 cap (if overnight)

🤖
Great news! Based on the details of your flight, you are eligible for expense reimbursement. Your flight AC 854 from London Heathrow to Toronto was cancelled and no alternative was offered within 24 hours. You were unable to travel for approximately 24 hours. Under the applicable EU regulations (EC 261/2004), you may be reimbursed for reasonable expenses incurred during this disruption.
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY
🤖
Based on the length of your disruption (24 hours), you may be eligible for reimbursement in the following categories: Meals: up to $60.00 Hotel accommodation: Rate of day (up to 3 nights, $500 maximum) Ground transportation: up to $200.00 Communication: up to $30.00 Parking: up to $50.00 (1 day maximum) Essential items: up to $30.00 Please note that all expenses must have been incurred during the disruption period and supported by valid receipts.
🤖
To process your claim, I'll need you to upload your receipts. Please upload photos or scans of your receipts for any expenses you incurred during the disruption.

⚙️ Currency conversion: £155 GBP → ~€182 EUR (for threshold comparison)
⚙️ Rate of day comparison: €182 vs rate of day (Heathrow area) → WITHIN range ✓
⚙️ OCR Result: PASS ✓
⚙️ Total meals: £48.00 GBP → ~€56 EUR

💬 Customer intent from test case
👤 My flight from Paris to Montreal took off but had to turn around and come back to Paris. The flight was cancelled after we returned. I was stuck at the airport for about 16 hours and had to pay for food, a hotel, and a taxi. Can I get reimbursed?
🗺️ Canonical Miro path — coverage (Standards of Care / SoC)
✓ asserted · ✕ required-by-Miro but missing · · not applicable

🤖
Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?
English
👤
🤖
Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice.
🤖
How can I help you today?
My flight from Paris to Montreal took off but had to turn around and come back to Paris. The flight was cancelled after we returned. I was stuck at the airport for about 16 hours and had to pay for food, a hotel, and a taxi. Can I get reimbursed?
👤
⚙️ the chatbot identifies Standards of Care (SoC) intent
⚙️ the chatbot checks: Is this a third-party submission? → NO
🤖
Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited. Would you like to get started?
🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.
🤖
Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable.
[First Name] [Last Name]
👤
🤖
Thanks, [First Name]! And, what is your booking reference number?
PNR: [PNR Code]
👤

🤖
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?
Email: t***@gmail.com
👤
🤖
I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it.

Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step.

I found the following journey: Flight: AC 856 Route: Paris Charles de Gaulle (CDG) → Montreal (YUL) Date: June 21, 2026 Status: DIVERTED — Returned to origin (CDG). Flight cancelled. Rebooked next day. Is this the journey you'd like to check?
Yes
👤
⚙️ the chatbot checks if there are more than 1 segment → No (single leg)
⚙️ the chatbot sends request to Case Services for duplicate check
⚙️ Case Services returns: NO existing case found for this passenger + journey combination

⚙️ the system calculates:
⚙️ Departing country: France → Limitation = 1,825 days (5 years)
⚙️ Days elapsed: Within limitation period → PROCEED

TRIAGE & DDS EVALUATION

⚙️ DDS Evaluates and sends to Trip Tracer UI:
TRIP TRACER UI VALIDATIONS
⚙️ the tester opens Trip Tracer UI for PNR
⚙️ ☐ Single-segment trip CDG→YUL on AC856 was diverted — returned to origin (CDG), then cancelled. Passenger returned to CDG, rebooked 16 hours later.
⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Flight: AC 856, Route: CDG → YUL, Scheduled Dep: 21-Jun 14:00
⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Flight: AC 856, Route: CDG → YUL, Status: DIVERTED — Returned to origin (CDG), CANCELLED, rebooked
⚙️ ☐ Diversion + cancellation ✓ | SOC Segment Delay: 960 min (16h) → ≥2h ✓
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-EU-EL-05
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
⚙️ the system sends case data to CyberSource for fraud screening (behind the scenes)
⚙️ CyberSource returns: GREEN (no fraud indicators detected)
⚙️ → PROCEED to eligible flow
🤖
Great news! Based on the details of your flight, you are eligible for expense reimbursement. Your flight AC 856 from Paris to Montreal was diverted back to Paris and subsequently cancelled. You were unable to travel for approximately 16 hours. Under the applicable EU regulations (EC 261/2004), you may be reimbursed for reasonable expenses incurred during this disruption.
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY
🤖
Based on the length of your disruption (16 hours), you may be eligible for reimbursement in the following categories: Meals: up to €60.00 Hotel accommodation: Rate of day (up to 3 nights, €500 maximum) Ground transportation: up to €200.00 Communication: up to €30.00 Parking: up to €50.00 (1 day maximum) Essential items: up to €30.00 Please note that all expenses must have been incurred during the disruption period and supported by valid receipts.
🤖
To process your claim, I'll need you to upload your receipts. Please upload photos or scans of your receipts for any expenses you incurred during the disruption.

receipt images:

⚙️ OCR Result: PASS ✓

🤖
Great news! Based on the details of your flight, you are eligible for expense reimbursement. Your connecting flight AC 859 from Toronto to Vancouver was cancelled, and you were stranded in Toronto for approximately 14 hours. Under the applicable EU regulations (EC 261/2004), you may be reimbursed for reasonable expenses incurred during this disruption.
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY
🤖
Based on the length of your disruption (14 hours), you may be eligible for reimbursement in the following categories: Meals: up to $60.00 Hotel accommodation: Rate of day (up to 3 nights, $500 maximum) Ground transportation: up to $200.00 Communication: up to $30.00 Parking: up to $50.00 (1 day maximum) Essential items: up to $30.00 Please note that all expenses must have been incurred during the disruption period and supported by valid receipts.
⚙️ Currency Note: Passenger stranded at YYZ (Canada) — receipts likely in CAD. Threshold comparison converts CAD → EUR. Payout in country of residence currency.
🤖
To process your claim, I'll need you to upload your receipts. Please upload photos or scans of your receipts for any expenses you incurred during the disruption.

Rebooked next day. • Delay Length: 840 min (14 hours)   • Controllability: NOT CHECKED – EU regime • Departure delay code: Flight cancelled (IRRELEVANT under EU SOC) • Passenger Type: Regular • Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Regular ✓ → 2. 💬 Customer intent from test case
👤 My Air Canada flight from Frankfurt to Toronto was delayed by about 8 hours because of a crew scheduling issue. I had to buy food, pay for a hotel, and take a taxi. Can I get reimbursed?

My Air Canada flight from Frankfurt to Toronto was delayed by about 8 hours because of a crew scheduling issue. I had to buy food, pay for a hotel, and take a taxi. Can I get reimbursed?

⚙️ the chatbot checks if there are more than 1 segment → No
⚙️ the chatbot sends request to Case Services for duplicate check

CLAIM TIME LIMIT CHECK
⚙️ the chatbot checks: Filing deadline for EU SOC per departing country
⚙️ the system calculates:
• Departing country: Germany → Limitation = 1,460 days (4 years)
• Days elapsed: Within limitation period → PROCEED
⚙️ the chatbot checks if there is triage data available? → Yes
TRIAGE & DDS EVALUATION
⚙️ the DDS evaluation is done in backend
⚙️ DDS Evaluates and sends to Trip Tracer UI:
TRIP TRACER UI VALIDATIONS
⚙️ the tester opens Trip Tracer UI for PNR
⚙️ ☐ Single-segment trip FRA→YYZ on AC837 was delayed +8H 00M due to weather (uncontrollable — NOT CHECKED under EU)
⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Flight: AC 837, Route: FRA → YYZ, Scheduled Dep: 22-Jun 10:00
⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Flight: AC 837, Route: FRA → YYZ, Status: ARRIVED LATE, Scheduled Dep: 10:00 → Actual: 17:00, Delay: +7H 00M (note: prereqs say 420 min/7h, chatbot says 8h — use prereqs)
🤖
being processed
⚙️ ☐ Post-fraud-clear: If cleared → Welcome Back email → receipt upload → normal flow
⚙️ ☐ Post-fraud-confirm: If confirmed fraud → rejection email → case closed
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-EU-EL-07
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
⚙️ the system sends case data to CyberSource for fraud screening (behind the scenes — not visible to passenger)
⚙️ CyberSource returns: YELLOW (potential fraud indicators detected)
• YELLOW means: suspicious but not confirmed fraud
• Case is PARKED in the CyberSource/Fraud queue
• Agent CANNOT see the CyberSource result — it's invisible to them
• The case appears in the fraud team's queue for review
• The passenger does NOT receive the Welcome Back email yet
• The passenger is NOT told about the fraud flag
WHAT THE PASSENGER SEES
⚙️ The chatbot does NOT mention fraud. Instead, it displays a generic processing message.
🤖
Thank you for your patience. We've reviewed the details of your flight AC 837 from Frankfurt to Toronto and confirmed your eligibility for expense reimbursement. Your case is currently being processed. You will receive an email with instructions to upload your receipts shortly.
🤖
shortly
WHAT HAPPENS BEHIND THE SCENES
• Case is created with ELIGIBLE status but PARKED in fraud queue
• Fraud team reviews the CyberSource indicators
• If fraud team CLEARS the case:
⚙️ → Welcome Back email sent to passenger
⚙️ → Passenger uploads receipts → normal flow continues
• If fraud team CONFIRMS fraud:
⚙️ → Rejection email sent to passenger
⚙️ → Case closed with CyberSource rejection status
⚙️ → Passenger can dispute → goes to manual handling
END FLOW (GLOB-11)
🤖
Is there anything else I can help you with?
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
No
👤
🤖
Thank you for choosing Air Canada.

📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify ELIGIBLE when customer traveled to destination with transit delay ≥2h under EU. Any disruption type qualifies (EU does not require controllability for SOC).

Prerequisites:
• Regime: EU
• Itinerary: Single segment — AC 837 FRA→YYZ
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 837 FRA→YYZ
• Promised Itinerary: AC 837 FRA→YYZ, Dep: 22-Jun 10:00
• Actual Itinerary: AC 837 FRA→YYZ, Dep: 22-Jun 17:00
• Departure Delay: 420 min (7 hours)
 
• Departure delay code: 77 (Weather) — Uncontrollable — NOT CHECKED under EU SOC (EU does not evaluate controllability; code present but irrelevant)
• Controllability: NOT CHECKED – EU does not evaluate controllability for SOC
• Passenger Type: Regular
• Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Regular ✓ → 2. Transit departure delay check → ≥2h → ELIGIBLE (no controllability check)
• PAX Count: 1 adult / Class: PY / Revenue: YES / ACR: No / Journey: > 72h
• Overnight Eligible: YES (delay >360 min)
• ENTITLEMENTS (, 1 adult, PY, no ACR): Meals: $30/pax (EUR equivalent) / Hotel: Rate of day, 3 nights max, $500 cap / Transport: $200 / Communication: $30 / Parking: $50 / Essentials: $30

🤖
Thank you for submitting your claim details. Your flight AC 848 from London Heathrow to Toronto arrived recently, and we need a little more time to gather all the necessary flight data to evaluate your eligibility. Your claim is being processed and you will receive an update within the next few days.
🤖
Here's what will happen next: We've created a reference for your claim: [Shell Case Reference] Our system will automatically evaluate your eligibility once all flight data is available (typically within 72 hours of your last flight) You will receive an email with a link to complete your claim if you are eligible Please keep any receipts for expenses you incurred during the disruption, as you may need to upload them later
⚙️ the system's polling queue triggers a re-evaluation
⚙️ the DDS evaluates eligibility with complete data:
⚙️ If ELIGIBLE: Deep link email sent to passenger → passenger clicks link → returns to chatbot → enters Welcome Back flow → uploads receipts → OCR validation → case creation → payment
⚙️ If NOT ELIGIBLE: Rejection email sent to passenger → case auto-closed
🤖

My flight from Tel Aviv to Toronto was delayed by about 6 hours. I had to buy food and pay for a hotel overnight. Can I get reimbursed for what I spent?

⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Flight: AC 084, Route: TLV → YYZ, Status: ARRIVED LATE, Scheduled Dep: 21:00 → Actual: 02-Jun 03:00, Delay: +6H 00M

I've reviewed the details of your booking. Unfortunately, your booking is classified as an employee booking, which does not qualify for Standards of Care expense reimbursement under the applicable regulations. If you incurred expenses during this disruption, please contact your employee services team for guidance on available options.

PAYMENT VALIDATION:
📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify NOT ELIGIBLE for AC employee booking under ASL regime.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: ASL
• Itinerary: Single segment — AC 084 TLV→YYZ
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 084 TLV→YYZ
• Promised Itinerary: AC 084 TLV→YYZ, Dep: 01-Jun 21:00
• Actual Itinerary: AC 084 TLV→YYZ, Dep: 02-Jun 03:00
• Departure Delay: 360 min (6 hours)
 
• Departure delay code: 64 (Flight Crew Shortage) — Controllable (present in flight data but never evaluated — employee check disqualifies first; ASL doesn't check controllability for SOC anyway)
• Controllability: N/A – employee not eligible regardless
• Passenger Type: Employee (AC) – office suffix ES/OT/EP/EE/EC
• Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Employee → NOT ELIGIBLE (no further checks)
• PAX Count: 1 adult
• Class of Booking: PY
• Revenue Booking: NO – Employee booking
• ACR Issued: N/A
• Journey Completed: > 72h
• Employee Flow: Employee is NOT blocked upfront. Goes through OTP authentication first, then receives "Not Eligible" response.
• Dispute Path: If disputed → case created → sent to manual handling.

TRIP TRACER UI VALIDATIONS
⚙️ the tester opens Trip Tracer UI for PNR
⚙️ ☐ Single-segment trip TLV→YYZ on AC084 was delayed +18H 00M — passenger notified 20 days before departure
⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Flight: AC 084, Route: TLV → YYZ, Scheduled Dep: 20-Jun 16:00
⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Flight: AC 084, Route: TLV → YYZ, Status: ARRIVED LATE, Scheduled Dep: 16:00 → Actual Dep: 21-Jun 10:00, Delay: +18H 00M
⚙️ ☐ Notification: 20 days before departure → ≥14 days threshold (ASL, same as EU) → NOT ELIGIBLE
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-ASL-NE-03
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
🤖
I've reviewed the details of your flight. Our records show that you were notified about the schedule change for flight AC 084 from Tel Aviv to Toronto approximately 18 days before your scheduled departure. Under the applicable Israeli aviation regulations (ASL), expense reimbursement is not available when passengers are notified of the disruption at least 14 days before the scheduled departure date, as this provides sufficient time to make alternative arrangements.
🤖
Do you agree with this assessment, or would you like to dispute it?
• Agree
• Dispute
Agree
👤

PAYMENT VALIDATION:
📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify NOT ELIGIBLE when customer informed ≥14 days before departure under ASL.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: ASL
• Itinerary: Single segment — AC 084 TLV→YYZ
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 084 TLV→YYZ
• Promised Itinerary: AC 084  TLV→YYZ, Dep: 20-Jun 16:00
• Actual Itinerary: AC 084  TLV→YYZ, Dep: 21-Jun 10:00
• Departure Delay: 1080 min (18 hours)
 
• Departure delay code: 64 (Flight Crew Shortage) — Controllable (present in flight data but never evaluated — notification check disqualifies first; ASL doesn't check controllability for SOC anyway)
• Controllability: Controllable (Code 64) — NOT EVALUATED (notification already disqualifies)
• Notification Time: 20 days before scheduled departure
• Passenger Type: Regular
• Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Regular ✓ → 2. Notification check → ≥14 days → NOT ELIGIBLE
• Note: ASL uses 14-day advance notification threshold (same as EU, different from APPR 12-hour)
• PAX Count: 1 adult
• Class of Booking: PY
• Revenue Booking: YES
• ACR Issued: N/A
• Journey Completed: > 72h

⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Flight: AC 084, Route: TLV → YYZ, Status: ARRIVED LATE, Scheduled Dep: 21:00 → Actual: 22:30, Delay: +1H 30M

⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
🤖
I've reviewed the details of your flight. Your flight AC 084 from Tel Aviv to Toronto was delayed by 1 hour and 30 minutes. Unfortunately, under the applicable Israeli aviation regulations (ASL), expense reimbursement is only available for delays of 2 hours or more. Since your delay was below this threshold, your claim is not eligible for reimbursement.
🤖

• Dispute
Agree

I've reviewed the details of your flight, and we confirm that this flight was operated by El Al Israel Airlines. Since Air Canada was not involved in operating this flight, we are unable to process a Standards of Care expense claim for this journey. Please reach out to El Al Israel Airlines directly for any claim-related requests regarding flight LY 001.

TRIAGE & DDS EVALUATION
⚙️ the DDS evaluation is done in backend
⚙️ DDS Evaluates and sends to Trip Tracer UI:
TRIP TRACER UI VALIDATIONS
⚙️ the tester opens Trip Tracer UI for PNR
⚙️ ☐ Single-segment trip LHR→TLV on AC080 was delayed +6H 00M — origin NOT in Israel (LHR = UK)
⚙️ ☐ Regime: EU (system determines — ASL does not apply, LHR departure → EU applies)
⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Flight: AC 080, Route: LHR → TLV, Scheduled Dep: 05-Jun 21:00
⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Flight: AC 080, Route: LHR → TLV, Status: ARRIVED LATE, Scheduled Dep: 21:00 → Actual: 06-Jun 03:00, Delay: +6H 00M
⚙️ ☐ Geographic Check (ASL): Origin = LHR (UK) — NOT Israel → ASL does NOT apply
⚙️ ☐ Destination = TLV (Israel) — PASS ✓ but origin fails → ASL FAIL
⚙️ ☐ Regime applied: EU (LHR = UK departure → EU/UK261 applies)
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-ASL-NE-06
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
🤖
I've reviewed the details of your flight. Your flight AC 080 departed from London (United Kingdom), which is outside Israel. Under the applicable Israeli aviation regulations (ASL), expense reimbursement is only available for flights departing from and arriving in Israel. Since your flight did not depart from Israel, your claim is not eligible under ASL regulations. However, your flight may be eligible under EU regulations (EC 261/2004) since it departed from the UK. Would you like us to check your eligibility under EU regulations instead?
🤖
Do you agree with this assessment, or would you like to dispute it?
• Agree
• Dispute
Agree
👤

TRIP TRACER UI VALIDATIONS
⚙️ the tester opens Trip Tracer UI for PNR
⚙️ ☐ Single-segment trip TLV→LHR on AC085 was delayed +7H 00M — destination NOT in Israel (LHR = UK)
⚙️ ☐ Regime: APPR (system determines — ASL does not apply, AC carrier → APPR applies)
⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Flight: AC 085, Route: TLV → LHR, Scheduled Dep: 05-Jun (time per prereqs)
⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Flight: AC 085, Route: TLV → LHR, Status: ARRIVED LATE, Delay: +7H 00M
⚙️ ☐ Geographic Check (ASL): Origin = TLV (Israel) — PASS ✓ | Destination = LHR (UK) — NOT Israel → ASL FAIL
⚙️ ☐ ASL requires BOTH origin AND destination in Israel → FAIL (destination not Israel)
⚙️ ☐ Regime applied: APPR (AC carrier → APPR applies)
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-ASL-NE-07
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
🤖
I've reviewed the details of your flight. Your flight AC 085 arrived in London (United Kingdom), which is outside Israel. Under the applicable Israeli aviation regulations (ASL), expense reimbursement is only available for flights departing from and arriving in Israel. Since your flight did not arrive in Israel, your claim is not eligible under ASL regulations. However, your flight may be eligible under Canadian regulations (APPR) since it was operated by Air Canada. Would you like us to check your eligibility under APPR instead?

🤖
Unfortunately, too much time has passed since your flight disruption to submit this claim. Your flight AC 084 from Tel Aviv to Eilat was disrupted on March 1, 2022 — approximately 1,561 days ago. Under the applicable Israeli aviation regulations (ASL), the filing deadline is 1,460 days (4 years) from the date of disruption. Since this deadline has passed, your claim is no longer eligible for expense reimbursement.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: ASL
• Itinerary: Single segment — AC 084 TLV→VDA

• Promised Itinerary: AC 084 YYZ→TLV, Dep: 01-Jan-2021 21:00
• Actual Itinerary: AC 084 YYZ→TLV, Dep: 02-Jan-2021 05:00
• Departure Delay: 480 min (8 hours)
 
• Departure delay code: 64 (Flight Crew Shortage) — Controllable (present in flight data but never evaluated — limitation check fails first)
• Controllability: Controllable (Code 64) — NOT EVALUATED (limitation expired)
• Passenger Type: Regular
• Check Order: 1. Limitation period check → >1460 days → EXPIRED → NOT ELIGIBLE
• Note: ASL limitation period = 1460 days (4 years) — different from APPR and EU
• Claim Filing Date: Beyond 1460 days from disruption
• PAX Count: 1 adult
• Class of Booking: PY
• Revenue Booking: YES
• ACR Issued: N/A
• Journey Completed: > 72h (well beyond)

  (but <2h for no-travel)
• Departure delay code: 64 (Flight Crew Shortage) — Controllable (present in flight data but never evaluated — delay threshold fails first; ASL doesn't check controllability for SOC anyway)
• Controllability: Controllable (Code 64) — NOT CHECKED (ASL does not evaluate controllability for SOC — same as EU)
• Passenger Type: Regular
• Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Regular ✓ → 2. No-travel SOC segment departure delay check → <2h → NOT ELIGIBLE
• Note: For no-travel scenarios under ASL, SOC segment departure delay must be ≥2h
• PAX Count: 1 adult
• Class of Booking: PY
• Revenue Booking: YES
• ACR Issued: N/A
• Journey Completed: > 72h

- Segment 1: AC 090 TLV→HFA (operated normally)
- Segment 2: AC 091 HFA→VDA (cancelled, rebooked)
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 091 HFA→VDA (Segment 2 — cancelled)
• Actual Itinerary: Incomplete journey. Stranded at mid-point (HFA). SOC segment departure delay = 170 min (2.82h).
• Departure Delay on SOC Segment: 170 min (2 hours 50 min)
  (but <2h for no-travel)
• Departure delay code: 64 (Flight Crew Shortage) — Controllable (present in flight data but never evaluated — delay threshold fails first; ASL doesn't check controllability for SOC anyway)
• Controllability: Controllable (Code 64) — NOT CHECKED (ASL does not evaluate controllability for SOC)
• Passenger Type: Regular
• Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Regular ✓ → 2. No-travel SOC segment departure delay check → <2h → NOT ELIGIBLE
• PAX Count: 1 adult / Class: PY / Revenue: YES / Journey: > 72h

I've reviewed the details of your flight. Our records show that flight AC 084 from Tel Aviv to Eilat operated as scheduled and was not cancelled. Since the flight was not disrupted, expense reimbursement is not available under the applicable Israeli aviation regulations (ASL). If you chose not to travel for personal reasons, you may want to review your ticket's fare rules for refund or rebooking options.

⚙️ I've reviewed the details of your flight. Our records show that flight AC 084 from Tel Aviv to Eilat operated and was not recorded as cancelled. Since the flight was not recorded as a cancellation in our system, expense reimbursement is not available under the applicable Israeli aviation regulations (ASL) for this journey. If you believe this assessment is incorrect — for example, if your flight was diverted back and you never reached your destination — you may dispute this decision and our team will review your case manually.

💬 Customer intent from test case
👤 I was on a connecting flight within Israel from Tel Aviv through Haifa to Eilat. My first leg was fine, but I got stuck in Haifa. The airline says my connecting flight from Haifa to Eilat wasn't cancelled — it just left without me because I arrived late from the first flight. I had to buy meals and stay overnight. Can I get reimbursed?

🤖
I've reviewed the details of your flight. Our records show that your connecting flight AC 091 from Haifa to Eilat operated and was not cancelled. Since the connecting flight was not recorded as a cancellation, expense reimbursement is not available under the applicable Israeli aviation regulations (ASL) for this journey. If you believe you missed your connection due to a delay on a previous Air Canada flight, you may dispute this decision and our team will review your case manually.
🤖

• Dispute

Since this segment was operated by another airline, we are unable to determine your eligibility for expense reimbursement at this time. We have created a case for our team to review manually. The disruption on your trip was caused by a delay on your Turkish Airlines flight TK 789 from Tel Aviv to Istanbul, which was operated by a Star Alliance partner. Since this segment was not operated by Air Canada, we are unable to determine your eligibility for expense reimbursement automatically at this time. We have created a case for our team to review. In the meantime, please have any receipts for expenses you incurred during the disruption ready, as our team may request them during the review.

💬 Customer intent from test case
👤 My Air Canada flight from Tel Aviv to Eilat was significantly delayed a couple of weeks ago. I had to pay for meals and accommodation. Can I get reimbursed?

⚙️ the chatbot checks has it been more than 72 hours for all segments in the selected journey → Yes (well beyond — disruption was May 16)
CLAIM TIME LIMIT CHECK
⚙️ the chatbot checks: Filing deadline for ASL SOC
⚙️ the system calculates:
• ASL limitation: 1,460 days (4 years) — FIXED
• Days elapsed: 24 days (disruption May 16, filing Jun 9) → Within limitation period → PROCEED
⚙️ the chatbot checks if there is triage data available? → Partial — operating data MISSING

⚙️ the DDS evaluation is done in backend
⚙️ DDS Evaluates and sends to Trip Tracer UI:
TRIP TRACER UI VALIDATIONS
⚙️ the tester opens Trip Tracer UI for PNR
⚙️ ☐ Single-segment trip TLV→VDA on AC084 was delayed +6H 00M — departure delay code missing after 14-day retrigger
⚙️ ☐ Regime: ASL (TLV→VDA = Israel→Israel)
⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Flight: AC 084, Route: TLV → VDA, Scheduled Dep: 15-May 16:00
⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Flight: AC 084, Route: TLV → VDA, Status: ARRIVED LATE, Scheduled Dep: 16:00 → Actual: 22:00, Delay: +6H 00M
⚙️ ☐ Geographic Check: TLV→VDA = Israel→Israel → PASSED ✓
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-ASL-ND-03
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
NO DETERMINATION RESULT
⚙️ I've reviewed the details of your flight AC 084 from Tel Aviv to Eilat. Unfortunately, we are unable to determine your eligibility for expense reimbursement automatically at this time because some of the required flight operating data is currently unavailable in our system. We have created a case for our team to investigate and review manually. You will receive an update once our team has completed their review.
CASE CREATION
⚙️ the system creates a case for manual agent review
⚙️ ASL regime — Operating data missing (actual dep/arr times) after 14-day RE retrigger. Cannot calculate delay. Geographic check passed (TLV→VDA = Israel→Israel). Agent must source operating data manually from operations team.
END FLOW (GLOB-11)

💬 Customer intent from test case
👤 I had a connecting flight booked through Air Canada from Tel Aviv through Haifa to Eilat. The Air Canada leg was fine, but the El Al leg from Haifa to Eilat was delayed and I missed my onward travel. I had to buy food and stay overnight. The airline says they still don't have the El Al flight data. Can I get reimbursed?
🗺️ Canonical Miro path — coverage (Standards of Care / SoC)
✓ asserted · ✕ required-by-Miro but missing · · not applicable
✓ GLOB-01✓ —✓ GenUC-23· GLOB-19 · ADD-112✓ GenUC-24✕ SOC-01✓ GenUC-01✓ GenUC-05✓ ADD-99✕ GenUC-06✕ GenUC-07✓ GenUC-08✓ GenUC-18✓ GenUC-10✓ SoC-02a · ADD-98✓ SoC-02a · GenUC-25 · ADD-83✓ ADD-105✓ SoC-02b· SOC-03c✕ SOC-03d· SOC-03e· SOC-03f✕ SOC-03g✓ SoC-03b · ADD-23 · GLOB-05✓ RR-01· SoC-03b· SoC-03b · ADD-27/106· GenUC-15✓ GenUC-16/19✓ GenUC-27✓ GLOB-11
⚠️ Gap analysis vs Miro · 1 med
MEDMulti-carrier itinerary: Miro shows a specific segment-matrix message (SOC-03c/d/e/f/g depending on AC / STAR / OAL mix). The corresponding message is not asserted.

Yes

⚙️ the chatbot checks if there are more than 1 segment → Yes (2 segments)
🤖
Which segment was affected by the disruption? • Segment 1: AC 090 TLV→HFA (Operated normally) • Segment 2: LY 301 HFA→VDA (Flight data unavailable)
⚙️ Segment 2: LY 301 HFA→VDA
⚙️ the chatbot sends request to Case Services for duplicate check
⚙️ Case Services returns: NO existing case found for this passenger + segment combination
⚙️ the chatbot checks if it passed the expected Departure time of the last itinerary → Yes
⚙️ the chatbot checks has it been more than 72 hours for all segments in the selected journey → Yes (well beyond — disruption was May 16)
CLAIM TIME LIMIT CHECK
⚙️ the chatbot checks: Filing deadline for ASL SOC
⚙️ the system calculates:
• ASL limitation: 1,460 days (4 years) — FIXED
• Days elapsed: 24 days (disruption May 16, filing Jun 9) → Within limitation period → PROCEED
⚙️ the chatbot checks if there is triage data available? → Partial — OAL operating data MISSING
TRIAGE & DDS EVALUATION
⚙️ the DDS evaluation is done in backend
⚙️ DDS Evaluates and sends to Trip Tracer UI:
TRIP TRACER UI VALIDATIONS
⚙️ the tester opens Trip Tracer UI for PNR
⚙️ ☐ Multi-segment trip TLV→HFA→VDA — Segment 1 AC090 on time, Segment 2 LY301 (OAL) — flight data unavailable after 14-day retrigger
⚙️ ☐ Regime: ASL (TLV→HFA→VDA = all Israel)
⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Seg 1: AC 090, TLV → HFA | Seg 2: LY 301, HFA → VDA
⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Seg 1: AC 090, TLV → HFA, Status: ON TIME | Seg 2: LY 301, HFA → VDA, Status: DATA UNAVAILABLE — OAL operating data not populated
⚙️ ☐ SOC section present — SOC Segment: LY 301 HFA→VDA (OAL)
⚙️ ☐ Geographic Check: TLV→HFA→VDA = all Israel → PASSED ✓
⚙️ ☐ Difference from ND-01: ND-01 = immediate OAL detection → instant ND. ND-05 = system waited 14 days, retriggered RE, OAL data still unavailable → ND after exhausting automated options.
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-ASL-ND-05
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC & OAL
NO DETERMINATION RESULT
⚙️ I've reviewed the details of your journey. The disruption appears to have occurred on your El Al flight LY 301 from Haifa to Eilat. Unfortunately, the operating data for this flight is currently unavailable in our system even after an extended data retrieval period, and we are unable to determine your eligibility for expense reimbursement automatically at this time. We have created a case for our team to investigate and review manually. You may also wish to contact El Al directly regarding any claims for this flight. You will receive an update once our team has completed their review.
CASE CREATION
⚙️ the system creates a case for manual agent review
⚙️ ASL regime — OAL (El Al LY 301) operating data unavailable after 14-day RE retrigger. Geographic check passed (TLV→HFA→VDA = all Israel). Agent must investigate OAL disruption data manually or coordinate with El Al for operating data. Passenger may also file directly with OAL.
END FLOW (GLOB-11)

⚙️ the difference from ND-01 is ND-01 detects OAL SOC immediately and triggers instant ND while ND-05 means the system WAITED 14 days, retriggered RE, STILL couldn't get OAL data so ND after exhausting all automated options

PAYMENT VALIDATION:
📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify NO DETERMINATION when OAL data unavailable after 14-day retrigger under ASL.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: ASL
• Itinerary: Multi-segment
- Segment 1: AC 090 TLV→HFA (operated normally — AC segment)
- Segment 2: LY 301 HFA→VDA (OAL data unavailable — El Al segment)
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): LY 301 HFA→VDA (Segment 2 — the OAL segment with suspected disruption)
• Departure Delay on SOC Segment: Unknown – OAL data unavailable
 Unknown
• Departure delay code: Unknown – OAL data unavailable
• Controllability: Unknown – OAL data unavailable; ASL doesn't check controllability for SOC anyway
• Passenger Type: Regular
• Check Order: 1. OAL data check → UNAVAILABLE → No Determination
• 14-Day RE Retrigger: Triggered and completed, OAL data still unavailable
• PAX Count: 1 adult / Class: PY / Revenue: YES / Journey: > 72h

• Regime: ASL
• Itinerary: Multi-segment
- Segment 1: AC 090 TLV→HFA (operated normally — AC segment)
- Segment 2: LY 301 HFA→VDA (OAL data unavailable — El Al segment)

• Departure Delay on SOC Segment: Unknown – OAL data unavailable

• Departure delay code: Unknown – OAL data unavailable

• 14-Day RE Retrigger: Triggered and completed, OAL data still unavailable

ASL EL h72 notravel star weather
💬 Customer intent from test case
👤 My Air Canada flight from Tel Aviv to Toronto was delayed by about 7 hours because of bad weather. I had to buy food, pay for a hotel overnight, and take a taxi. Can I get reimbursed for what I spent?
🗺️ Canonical Miro path — coverage (Standards of Care / SoC)
✓ asserted · ✕ required-by-Miro but missing · · not applicable
✓ GLOB-01✓ —✓ GenUC-23· GLOB-19 · ADD-112✓ GenUC-24✓ SOC-01✓ GenUC-01✓ GenUC-05✓ ADD-99· GenUC-06· GenUC-07✓ GenUC-08✓ GenUC-18✓ GenUC-10✓ SoC-02a · ADD-98✓ SoC-02a · GenUC-25 · ADD-83✓ ADD-105✓ SoC-02b✕ SOC-03c· SOC-03d✕ SOC-03e· SOC-03f· SOC-03g✓ SoC-03b · ADD-23 · GLOB-05✓ RR-01✓ SoC-03b✓ SoC-03b · ADD-27/106· GenUC-15✓ GenUC-16/19✕ GenUC-27✓ GLOB-11
⚠️ Gap analysis vs Miro · 1 med
MEDMulti-carrier itinerary: Miro shows a specific segment-matrix message (SOC-03c/d/e/f/g depending on AC / STAR / OAL mix). The corresponding message is not asserted.
💬 Test-case conversation from test case (Detailed Steps)
🤖
Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?
English
👤
🤖
Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice.
🤖
How can I help you today?
My Air Canada flight from Tel Aviv to Toronto was delayed by about 7 hours because of bad weather. I had to buy food, pay for a hotel overnight, and take a taxi. Can I get reimbursed for what I spent?
👤
⚙️ the chatbot identifies Standards of Care (SoC) intent
⚙️ the chatbot checks: Is this a third-party submission? → NO
🤖
Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited. Would you like to get started?
🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.
🤖
Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable.
[First Name] [Last Name]
👤
🤖
Thanks, [First Name]! And, what is your booking reference number?
PNR: [PNR Code]
👤
⚙️ the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR: [PNR Code]
⚙️ Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND
🤖
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?
• Email: t***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***789
Email: t***@gmail.com
👤
🤖
I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it.
546879
👤
⚙️ the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
🤖
Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step.
⚙️ the chatbot checks if this PNR a group booking → No
⚙️ the chatbot checks if there are multiple PAX on PNR? → No
🤖
I found the following journey: • Flight: AC 084 • Route: Tel Aviv (TLV) → Toronto (YYZ) • Date: June 10, 2026 • Departure Delay: 7 hours Is this the journey you'd like to check?
Yes
👤
⚙️ the chatbot checks if there are more than 1 segment → No
⚙️ the chatbot sends request to Case Services for duplicate check
⚙️ Case Services returns: NO existing case found for this passenger + journey combination
⚙️ the chatbot checks if it passed the expected Departure time of the last itinerary → Yes
⚙️ the chatbot checks has it been more than 72 hours for all segments in the selected journey → Yes
CLAIM TIME LIMIT CHECK
⚙️ the chatbot checks: Filing deadline for ASL SOC
⚙️ the system calculates:
• ASL limitation: 1,460 days (4 years) — FIXED
• Days elapsed: Within limitation period → PROCEED
⚙️ the chatbot checks if there is triage data available? → Yes
TRIAGE & DDS EVALUATION
⚙️ the DDS evaluation is done in backend
⚙️ DDS Evaluates and sends to Trip Tracer UI:
TRIP TRACER UI VALIDATIONS
⚙️ the tester opens Trip Tracer UI for PNR
⚙️ ☐ Single-segment trip TLV→YYZ on AC085 was delayed +7H 00M (note: prereqs say 24h/1440 min but chatbot says 7h — use chatbot flow: 7h) due to weather (uncontrollable — NOT CHECKED under ASL)
⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Flight: AC 085, Route: TLV → YYZ, Scheduled Dep: 18-Jun 16:00
⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Flight: AC 085, Route: TLV → YYZ, Status: ARRIVED LATE, Delay: +7H 00M
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-ASL-EL-01
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
⚙️ the system sends case data to CyberSource for fraud screening (behind the scenes — not visible to passenger)
⚙️ CyberSource returns: GREEN (no fraud indicators detected)
⚙️ → PROCEED to eligible flow
🤖
Great news! Based on the details of your flight, you are eligible for expense reimbursement. Your flight AC 084 from Tel Aviv to Eilat was delayed by approximately 7 hours. Under the applicable Israeli aviation regulations (ASL), you may be reimbursed for reasonable expenses incurred during this disruption.
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY
🤖
Based on the length of your disruption (7 hours), you may be eligible for reimbursement in the following categories: • Meals: up to ₪110.00 • Hotel accommodation: Rate of day (up to 3 nights, ₪1,800 maximum) • Ground transportation: up to ₪720.00 • Communication: up to ₪110.00 • Parking: up to ₪180.00 (1 day maximum) • Essential items: up to ₪110.00
ASL CURRENCY NOTE
⚙️ 1. Receipt currency → regime currency (ILS) for threshold comparison
⚙️ 2. Regime currency → country of residence currency for payout display
🤖
RECEIPT UPLOAD — WELCOME BACK FLOW:
🤖
To process your claim, I'll need you to upload your receipts. Please upload photos or scans of your receipts for any expenses you incurred during the disruption.
⚙️ the chatbot displays the receipt upload interface
receipt images:
👤
• Receipt 1: Hotel receipt — ₪550.00 ILS (1 night near Ben Gurion)
• Receipt 2: Meal receipt — ₪95.00 ILS (dinner + breakfast)
• Receipt 3: Taxi receipt — ₪180.00 ILS (airport to hotel + hotel to airport)
• Receipt 4: Communication — ₪45.00 ILS (Wi-Fi + phone calls)
⚙️ RECEIPT READER — OCR VALIDATION (RR-01):
⚙️ the system processes uploaded receipts through OCR (Agentic Receipt Reader)
⚙️ the OCR engine extracts line items from each receipt:
• Line item: Hotel stay, 1 night — ₪550.00 ILS
• Date: June 10, 2026
• Category: Hotel
• Within disruption window: YES ✓
• Rate of day comparison: ₪550 vs rate of day (Tel Aviv area) → WITHIN range ✓
• Nights: 1 (within 3-night max) ✓
• Amount: ₪550 < ₪1,800 cap ✓
• OCR Result: PASS ✓
• Line item 1: Dinner — ₪55.00 ILS
• Line item 2: Breakfast — ₪40.00 ILS
• Total meals: ₪95.00 ILS
• Date: June 10-11, 2026
• Category: Meals
• Within disruption window: YES ✓
• Threshold check: ₪95 ≤ ₪110 ( meal entitlement) ✓
• OCR Result: PASS ✓
• Line item 1: Taxi airport→hotel — ₪100.00 ILS
• Line item 2: Taxi hotel→airport — ₪80.00 ILS
• Total transport: ₪180.00 ILS
• Date: June 10-11, 2026
• Category: Transport
• Within disruption window: YES ✓
• Threshold check: ₪180 ≤ ₪720 ✓
• OCR Result: PASS ✓
• Line item 1: Wi-Fi — ₪25.00 ILS
• Line item 2: Phone calls — ₪20.00 ILS
• Total communication: ₪45.00 ILS
• Date: June 10, 2026
• Category: Communication
• Within disruption window: YES ✓
• Threshold check: ₪45 ≤ ₪110 ✓
• OCR Result: PASS ✓
RECEIPT VALIDATION SUMMARY
⚙️ the system displays the OCR validation summary:
• Total receipts processed: 4
• Total line items extracted: 6
• Items PASSED: 6
• Items FLAGGED for agent review: 0
• Items FAILED: 0
EXPENSE BREAKDOWN
• Hotel: ₪550.00 ✓
• Meals: ₪95.00 ✓
• Transport: ₪180.00 ✓
• Communication: ₪45.00 ✓
• Parking: ₪0.00 (no receipt submitted)
• Essentials: ₪0.00 (no receipt submitted)
• TOTAL APPROVED: ₪870.00 ILS
CASE CREATION (GLOB-09)
⚙️ the system creates a case in Case Services
• DDS response code: SoC-ASL-EL-01
• Eligibility: ELIGIBLE
• Regime: ASL
• Currency: ILS (receipts in ILS — no conversion needed for threshold comparison)
• All approved line items with amounts
• Receipt images attached
• Fraud screening result: GREEN
• Note: Weather disruption (Code 77) — APPR would reject as uncontrollable, but ASL does NOT check controllability → ELIGIBLE (same as EU)
🤖
Your claim has been submitted successfully. Here's a summary: • Case Reference: [Case Number] • Total claim amount: ₪870.00 ILS • Categories: Hotel (₪550.00), Meals (₪95.00), Transport (₪180.00), Communication (₪45.00) You will receive a confirmation email shortly with your case details and next steps.
⚙️ the system triggers notification via Adobe
• Case reference number
• Claim summary with approved amounts (in country of residence currency)
• Next steps for payment processing
CLAIMS PAYMENT FLOW
⚙️ the system initiates payment processing
• Approved amount: ₪870.00 ILS
• Currency conversion to country of residence currency (if different from ILS)
• Payment method determination based on country of residence:
⚙️ - Israel → Local bank transfer or BSM
⚙️ - Canada → EFT (converted to CAD)
⚙️ - US + 70 BSM countries → VSF
⚙️ - Others → BSM or manual
🤖

• Country of residence: [Country]
• Payment method: [determined by country]
• Payment details: [collected securely]

Your payment of [amount in local currency] will be processed via [payment method]. You should receive the funds within [X] business days.
END FLOW (GLOB-11)

📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify ELIGIBLE when customer stayed at origin,  delay ≥2h, ASL regime. Full eligible flow.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: ASL •  Carrier: AC (AC 085 TLV→YYZ) • Promised Itinerary: AC 085 TLV→YYZ, Dep: 18-Jun 16:00, Arr: 18-Jun 22:00 • Actual Itinerary: Flight cancelled. Rebooked next day. Passenger stayed at origin (TLV). • Delay Length: 1440 min (24 hours)   • Controllability: NOT CHECKED – ASL does not evaluate controllability for SOC • Departure delay code: 77 (Weather – IRRELEVANT under ASL SOC) • Passenger Type: Regular • Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Regular ✓ → 2. No-travel delay check → ≥2h → ELIGIBLE (no controllability check) • Note: ASL regime = eligible regardless of controllability (same as EU) • Geographic Check: Origin = TLV (Israel) ✓ | Destination = YYZ → itinerary starts in Israel ✓ • PAX Count: 1 adult • Class of Booking: PY • Revenue Booking: YES • ACR Issued: Hotel: No, Transport: No, Meal: No • Journey Completed: > 72h ENTITLEMENTS (, 1 adult, PY, no ACR): • Meals: $60/pax (converted to ILS for ASL regime) • Hotel: Rate of day, max 3 nights, $500 cap • Transport: $200 (1-5 pax) • Communication: $30/pax • Parking: $50, 1 day max • Essentials: $30/pax

• Regime: ASL •  Carrier: AC (AC 085 TLV→YYZ) • Promised Itinerary: AC 085 TLV→YYZ, Dep: 18-Jun 16:00, Arr: 18-Jun 22:00 • Actual Itinerary: Flight cancelled. Rebooked next day. Passenger stayed at origin (TLV). • Delay Length: 1440 min (24 hours)   • Controllability: NOT CHECKED – ASL does not evaluate controllability for SOC • Departure delay code: 77 (Weather – IRRELEVANT under ASL SOC) • Passenger Type: Regular • Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Regular ✓ → 2. No-travel delay check → ≥2h → ELIGIBLE (no controllability check) • Note: ASL regime = eligible regardless of controllability (same as EU) • Geographic Check: Origin = TLV (Israel) ✓ | Destination = YYZ → itinerary starts in Israel ✓ • PAX Count: 1 adult • Class of Booking: PY • Revenue Booking: YES • ACR Issued: Hotel: No, Transport: No, Meal: No • Journey Completed: > 72h ENTITLEMENTS (, 1 adult, PY, no ACR): • Meals: $60/pax (converted to ILS for ASL regime) • Hotel: Rate of day, max 3 nights, $500 cap • Transport: $200 (1-5 pax) • Communication: $30/pax • Parking: $50, 1 day max • Essentials: $30/pax

🤖
Great news! Based on the details of your flight, you are eligible for expense reimbursement. Your flight AC 084 from Tel Aviv to Eilat was cancelled and you were unable to travel for approximately 18 hours. Under the applicable Israeli aviation regulations (ASL), you may be reimbursed for reasonable expenses incurred during this disruption.
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY
🤖
Based on the length of your disruption (18 hours), you may be eligible for reimbursement in the following categories: • Meals: up to ₪220.00 • Hotel accommodation: Rate of day (up to 3 nights, ₪1,800 maximum) • Ground transportation: up to ₪720.00 • Communication: up to ₪110.00 • Parking: up to ₪180.00 (1 day maximum) • Essential items: up to ₪110.00 Please note that all expenses must have been incurred during the disruption period and supported by valid receipts.
🤖
To process your claim, I'll need you to upload your receipts. Please upload photos or scans of your receipts for any expenses you incurred during the disruption.
⚙️ the chatbot displays the receipt upload interface
receipt images:
👤
• Receipt 1: Hotel receipt — ₪600.00 ILS (1 night near Ben Gurion)
• Receipt 2: Meal receipt — ₪190.00 ILS (dinner + breakfast + lunch)
• Receipt 3: Taxi receipt — ₪200.00 ILS (airport to hotel + hotel to airport)
• Receipt 4: Essential items — ₪95.00 ILS (toiletries)
⚙️ RECEIPT READER — OCR VALIDATION (RR-01):
⚙️ the system processes uploaded receipts through OCR (Agentic Receipt Reader)
⚙️ the OCR engine extracts line items from each receipt:
• Line item: Hotel stay, 1 night — ₪600.00 ILS
• Date: June 11, 2026
• Category: Hotel
• Within disruption window: YES ✓
• Rate of day comparison: ₪600 vs rate of day (Tel Aviv area) → WITHIN range ✓
• Nights: 1 (within 3-night max) ✓

ASL EL h72 multipax multiseg notravel
💬 Customer intent from test case
👤 I was on a connecting flight within Israel from Tel Aviv through Haifa to Eilat. My first leg was fine, but my second leg from Haifa to Eilat was cancelled because of an aircraft defect. I was stuck in Haifa for about 3 hours. I had to buy food while waiting. Can I get reimbursed?
🗺️ Canonical Miro path — coverage (Standards of Care / SoC)
✓ asserted · ✕ required-by-Miro but missing · · not applicable
✓ GLOB-01✓ —✓ GenUC-23· GLOB-19 · ADD-112✓ GenUC-24✓ SOC-01✓ GenUC-01✓ GenUC-05✓ ADD-99✕ GenUC-06✕ GenUC-07✓ GenUC-08✓ GenUC-18✓ GenUC-10✓ SoC-02a · ADD-98✓ SoC-02a · GenUC-25 · ADD-83✓ ADD-105✓ SoC-02b· SOC-03c· SOC-03d· SOC-03e· SOC-03f· SOC-03g✓ SoC-03b · ADD-23 · GLOB-05✓ RR-01✓ SoC-03b✓ SoC-03b · ADD-27/106· GenUC-15✓ GenUC-16/19· GenUC-27✓ GLOB-11
⚠️ Gap analysis vs Miro
✅ No divergences detected against the Miro flow (heuristic — confirm against the canvas).
💬 Test-case conversation from test case (Detailed Steps)
🤖
Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?
English
👤
🤖
Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice.
🤖
How can I help you today?
I was on a connecting flight within Israel from Tel Aviv through Haifa to Eilat. My first leg was fine, but my second leg from Haifa to Eilat was cancelled because of an aircraft defect. I was stuck in Haifa for about 3 hours. I had to buy food while waiting. Can I get reimbursed?
👤
⚙️ the chatbot identifies Standards of Care (SoC) intent
⚙️ the chatbot checks: Is this a third-party submission? → NO
🤖
Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited. Would you like to get started?
🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.
🤖
Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable.
[First Name] [Last Name]
👤
🤖
Thanks, [First Name]! And, what is your booking reference number?
PNR: [PNR Code]
👤

⚙️ Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND
🤖
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?
• Email: t***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***789
Email: t***@gmail.com
👤
🤖
I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it.
546879
👤
⚙️ the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
🤖
Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step.
⚙️ the chatbot checks if this PNR a group booking → No
⚙️ the chatbot checks if there are multiple PAX on PNR? → No
🤖
I found the following journey: • Flight: AC 090 → AC 091 • Route: Tel Aviv (TLV) → Haifa (HFA) → Eilat (VDA) • Date: June 13, 2026 • Departure Delay: 180 min (3 hours) Is this the journey you'd like to check?
Yes
👤
⚙️ the chatbot checks if there are more than 1 segment → Yes (2 segments)
🤖
Which segment was affected by the disruption? * Segment 1: AC 090 TLV→HFA (Operated normally) * Segment 2: AC 091 HFA→VDA (Cancelled)
⚙️ Segment 2: AC 091 HFA→VDA
⚙️ the chatbot sends request to Case Services for duplicate check
⚙️ Case Services returns: NO existing case found for this passenger + journey combination
⚙️ the chatbot checks if it passed the expected Departure time of the last itinerary → Yes
⚙️ the chatbot checks has it been more than 73 hours for all segments in the selected journey → Yes
CLAIM TIME LIMIT CHECK
⚙️ the chatbot checks: Filing deadline for ASL SOC
⚙️ the system calculates:
• ASL limitation: 1,460 days (4 years) — FIXED
• Days elapsed: Within limitation period → PROCEED
⚙️ the chatbot checks if there is triage data available? → Yes
TRIAGE & DDS EVALUATION
⚙️ the DDS evaluation is done in backend
⚙️ DDS Evaluates and sends to Trip Tracer UI:
TRIP TRACER UI VALIDATIONS
⚙️ the tester opens Trip Tracer UI for PNR
⚙️ ☐ Multi-segment trip TLV→HFA→VDA — Segment 2 AC091 HFA→VDA was cancelled due to aircraft defect. Passenger stranded at HFA for 3 hours.
⚙️ ☐ Regime: ASL (TLV→HFA→VDA = all Israel)
⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Seg 1: AC 090, TLV → HFA | Seg 2: AC 091, HFA → VDA
⚙️ ☐ Current Itinerary — Seg 1: AC 090, TLV → HFA, Status: ON TIME | Seg 2: AC 091, HFA → VDA, Status: CANCELLED
⚙️ ☐ SOC section present — SOC Segment: AC 091 HFA→VDA
⚙️ ☐ Geographic Check: TLV→HFA→VDA = all Israel → PASSED ✓
⚙️ ☐ Cancellation: YES ✓ | SOC Segment Delay: 180 min (3h) → ≥2h ✓
⚙️ DDS RESPONSE CODE = SoC-ASL-EL-03
⚙️ the chatbot checks which airline company is it → AC
⚙️ the system sends case data to CyberSource for fraud screening (behind the scenes — not visible to passenger)
⚙️ CyberSource returns: GREEN (no fraud indicators detected)
⚙️ → PROCEED to eligible flow

Your connecting flight AC 091 from Haifa to Eilat was cancelled due to an aircraft defect, and you were stranded in Haifa for approximately 3 hours.
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY
🤖
Based on the length of your disruption (3 hours), you may be eligible for reimbursement in the following categories: • Meals: up to ₪55.00 • Communication: up to ₪110.00 Please note that due to the length of the disruption (under 6 hours), hotel accommodation, ground transportation, parking, and essential items are not eligible for reimbursement at this time. All expenses must have been incurred during the disruption period and supported by valid receipts.

To process your claim, I'll need you to upload your receipts. Please upload photos or scans of your receipts for any expenses you incurred during the disruption.

⚙️ ☐ Promised Itinerary — Flight: AC 084, Route: TLV → VDA, Scheduled Dep: 20-Jun 16:00

🤖
Great news! Based on the details of your flight, you are eligible for expense reimbursement. Your flight AC 084 from Tel Aviv to Eilat was cancelled and you were unable to travel for approximately 14 hours. Under the applicable Israeli aviation regulations (ASL), you may be reimbursed for reasonable expenses incurred during this disruption.
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY
🤖
Based on the length of your disruption (14 hours), you may be eligible for reimbursement in the following categories: • Meals: up to ₪220.00 • Hotel accommodation: Rate of day (up to 3 nights, ₪1,800 maximum) • Ground transportation: up to ₪720.00 • Communication: up to ₪110.00 • Parking: up to ₪180.00 (1 day maximum) • Essential items: up to ₪110.00 Please note that all expenses must have been incurred during the disruption period and supported by valid receipts.
🤖
To process your claim, I'll need you to upload your receipts. Please upload photos or scans of your receipts for any expenses you incurred during the disruption.

💬 Customer intent from test case
👤 My flight from Tel Aviv to Eilat took off but had to turn around and come back to Ben Gurion airport because of a mechanical problem. I was stuck at the airport for about 16 hours and had to pay for food, a hotel, and a taxi. Can I get reimbursed?

✓ asserted · ✕ required-by-Miro but missing · · not applicable

Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?
English
👤

Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice.
🤖
How can I help you today?
My flight from Tel Aviv to Eilat took off but had to turn around and come back to Ben Gurion airport because of a mechanical problem. I was stuck at the airport for about 16 hours and had to pay for food, a hotel, and a taxi. Can I get reimbursed?
👤

⚙️ the chatbot checks: Is this a third-party submission? → NO
🤖
Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited. Would you like to get started?
🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.
🤖

🤖
Great news! Based on the details of your flight, you are eligible for expense reimbursement. Your connecting flight AC 091 from Haifa to Eilat was cancelled due to a crew scheduling issue, and you were stranded in Haifa for approximately 12 hours. Under the applicable Israeli aviation regulations (ASL), you may be reimbursed for reasonable expenses incurred during this disruption.
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY
🤖
Based on the length of your disruption (12 hours), you may be eligible for reimbursement in the following categories: • Meals: up to ₪220.00 • Hotel accommodation: Rate of day (up to 3 nights, ₪1,800 maximum) • Ground transportation: up to ₪720.00 • Communication: up to ₪110.00 • Parking: up to ₪180.00 (1 day maximum) • Essential items: up to ₪110.00 Please note that all expenses must have been incurred during the disruption period and supported by valid receipts.
🤖
To process your claim, I'll need you to upload your receipts.

receipt images:

PAYMENT VALIDATION:
📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify ELIGIBLE when customer traveled to destination with transit delay ≥2h under ASL. Any disruption type qualifies.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: ASL
• Itinerary: Single segment — AC 084 TLV→VDA
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 084 TLV→VDA
• Promised Itinerary: AC 084 TLV→VDA, Dep: 22-Jun 16:00
• Actual Itinerary: AC 084 TLV→VDA, Dep: 22-Jun 23:00
• Departure Delay: 420 min (7 hours)
 
• Departure delay code: 77 (Weather) — Uncontrollable — NOT CHECKED under ASL SOC (ASL does not evaluate controllability; code present but irrelevant)
• Controllability: NOT CHECKED – ASL does not evaluate controllability for SOC
• Passenger Type: Regular
• Check Order: 1. Pax Type → Regular ✓ → 2. Transit departure delay check → ≥2h → ELIGIBLE (no controllability check)
• Note: SAME AS EU — controllability not checked. Under APPR, weather + regular pax = NOT ELIGIBLE (NE-07). Under ASL = ELIGIBLE.
• Geographic Check: Origin = TLV (Israel) ✓ | Destination = VDA (Israel) ✓ → BOTH Israel → PASSED ✓
• PAX Count: 1 adult / Class: PY / Revenue: YES / ACR: No / Journey: > 72h
• Overnight Eligible: YES (delay >360 min)
• ENTITLEMENTS (, 1 adult, PY, no ACR): Meals ₪110 / Hotel: Rate of day, ₪1,800 cap / Transport ₪720 / Communication ₪110 / Parking ₪180 / Essentials ₪110

Thank you for submitting your claim details. Your flight AC 084 from Tel Aviv to Eilat arrived recently, and we need a little more time to gather all the necessary flight data to evaluate your eligibility. Your claim is being processed and you will receive an update within the next few days.
🤖
Here's what will happen next: • We've created a reference for your claim: [Shell Case Reference] • Our system will automatically evaluate your eligibility once all flight data is available (typically within 72 hours of your last flight) • You will receive an email with a link to complete your claim if you are eligible • Please keep any receipts for expenses you incurred during the disruption, as you may need to upload them later
⚙️ the system's polling queue triggers a re-evaluation

• Geographic check: TLV→VDA = Israel→Israel — confirmed

• Regime: ASL (TLV→VDA = Israel→Israel confirmed)
• Geographic check: PASSED ✓ (both origin and destination in Israel)
• Controllability: NOT CHECKED (ASL regime)
• Delay: confirmed
• If ELIGIBLE: Deep link email sent to passenger → passenger clicks link → returns to chatbot → enters Welcome Back flow → uploads receipts → OCR validation → case creation → payment
• If NOT ELIGIBLE: Rejection email sent to passenger → case auto-closed
• If NO DETERMINATION: Case moved to manual agent queue

• Actual cause: Crew scheduling (Code 41) — CONTROLLABLE

🤖
Good news! After reviewing your dispute for flight AC 201 from Toronto to Calgary on June 3, 2026, we have determined that you are eligible for expense reimbursement. Please click the link below to complete your claim by uploading your receipts.
• Deep link to chatbot with pre-populated claim data
• Case reference number
• Expiry date for completing the claim
⚙️ the chatbot opens with pre-populated data:
🤖
Welcome back, [First Name]! We've reviewed your dispute and determined that you are eligible for expense reimbursement for your flight AC 201 from Toronto to Calgary on June 3, 2026. To complete your claim, please upload your receipts.
ENTITLEMENT DISPLAY
🤖
Based on the length of your disruption (6 hours), you may be eligible for reimbursement in the following categories: • Meals: up to $30.00 • Hotel accommodation: Rate of day (up to 3 nights, $500 maximum) • Ground transportation: up to $200.00 • Communication: up to $30.00 • Parking: up to $50.00 (1 day maximum) • Essential items: up to $30.00 Please note that all expenses must have been incurred during the disruption period and supported by valid receipts.
RECEIPT UPLOAD
🤖
To process your claim, I'll need you to upload your receipts. Please upload photos or scans of your receipts for any expenses you incurred during the disruption.
⚙️ the chatbot displays the receipt upload interface
receipt images:

• Receipt 2: Meal receipt — $28.00 CAD (dinner)

• Rate of day comparison: $170 vs rate of day → WITHIN range ✓
• Nights: 1 (within 3-night max) ✓
• Amount: $170 < $500 cap ✓
• OCR Result: PASS ✓
• Line item: Dinner — $28.00 CAD
• Date: June 3, 2026
• Category: Meals
• Within disruption window: YES ✓
• Threshold check: $28 ≤ $30 ( meal entitlement) ✓
• OCR Result: PASS ✓
• Line item 1: Taxi airport→hotel — $25.00 CAD
• Line item 2: Taxi hotel→airport — $20.00 CAD
• Total transport: $45.00 CAD
• Date: June 3-4, 2026
• Category: Transport
• Within disruption window: YES ✓
• Threshold check: $45 ≤ $200 ✓
• OCR Result: PASS ✓

EXPECTED RESULTS
⚙️ SoC-Override-Pay. ELIGIBLE. Emergency override resolved to Pay. Normal DDS evaluation skipped — override takes precedence. Override amounts applied (may differ from standard entitlements). Fraud screening GREEN. Full receipt upload + OCR + case creation + notification. Case flagged with override metadata. Even if normal DDS would have returned NOT ELIGIBLE, override Pay = ELIGIBLE.

PAYMENT VALIDATION:
📋 Prerequisites / Description
Verify that when an emergency override results in Pay, the system returns ELIGIBLE per override amounts and proceeds to full receipt upload and case creation.

Prerequisites:
• Regime: ANY (Override applies across all regimes)
• Itinerary: Single segment — AC 201 YYZ→YYC
• SOC Segment (per SoC Flight Identifier): AC 201 YYZ→YYC
• Promised Itinerary: AC 201 YYZ→YYC, Dep: 27-Jun 07:00
• Actual Itinerary: AC 201 YYZ→YYC, Dep: 27-Jun 15:00
• Departure Delay: 480 min (8 hours)
 
• Original Departure delay code: 77 (Weather) — originally coded as weather
• Corrected Departure delay code (post-override): 64 (Flight Crew Shortage) — Controllable — investigation reveals weather code was incorrectly assigned; actual cause was flight crew shortage
• Override Status: RESOLVED – Pay
• Override Reason: Departure delay code was MISCLASSIFIED. Weather (77) was incorrectly assigned; actual cause was Flight Crew Shortage (64 — Controllable). Agent corrects after investigation.
• Passenger Type: Regular
• Check Order: 1. Normal DDS → NE-07 (weather, regular pax) → 2. Dispute → 3. Agent investigation → 4. Override APPROVED → PAY → ELIGIBLE
• PAX Count: 1 adult / Class: PY / Revenue: YES / ACR: No / Journey: > 72h
• ENTITLEMENTS (, standard APPR — post-override): Meals $30 / Hotel: Rate of day, $500 cap / Transport $200 / Communication $30 / Parking $50 / Essentials $30
